properties.review_text_published:
The place is managed and run by a group of bubbly, warm and kind women! Shout out to Vaso 🔥🤩!

I had the tarama with crab: beautiful presentation, the texture on the dish are out of control! Crunchy, smooth, chewy from the crab and veggies.

The sea bass sushi is interesting: wrapped in grape leaves, vegetables inside, homemade siracha mayo. The textures and flavors outta control again 🔥🎉

Finally, the cod with cauliflower puree and celery/carrots. The ingredients all spoke loudly in this one! The leeks, moist and sweet, the carrots all dente, the cauliflower tangy and the cod 🤯🎉🔥

A cozy and warm tavern, with a relaxed vibe and energy: Thessaloniki is built different compared to Athens pace wise. The vibe in this tavern perfectly embodies that sentiment.

I was greeted by a very warm lady and was presented the menu and specials by an equally warm and polite young man.

I had the grilled eggplants, grilled pepper and gnocchi with lamb and lemon sauce.

Eggplant grilled to perfection: char grilled, flaky on the outside, moist and smooth in the inside.

The lamb and gnocchi wow 😲😳🤯: super creative and tasty. The lamb was cooked to perfection, moist, tasting the herbs and lemon 🍋 it was cooked in. Tangy enough. Just the right amount. The gnocchi were also fire 🔥! Amazing.

So much attention and care too 😘, but not the overbearing/intrusive kind. Just love.

Greeks know how to do it, baby!

Look at this cold cut plate, come on now...🔥😉.

Wine list primarily from the big estates, Gerovasiliou, Kir Yanni, Alpha.

I would have loved to sip some from the smaller ones around: Dalamara, Founti, Thymiopoulos...🙏🏿🔥.

Shout out to Konstantinos the bar tender and tonthr young lady that worked with him! On request he made me this mastiha cocktail! 🍸🙏🏿.

I was glad to stay at Smooth, in the heart of down town Thessaloniki, near many great restaurants and bar. Despite being in a central location, it was quiet, especially at night.

I was hosted by Elias, a young man who studied in the US.

Elias responded with minutes to the first email I sent him.

After I made my booking, he was very kind to share a number I could exchange with him on using WhatsApp.

He is a very courteous person who is ready, willing and able to come to your assistance.

As you can see, the room is spotless, well lit and very clean. Communication was very clear and attentive.

If you're driving, there is parking a 3 minute walk away.

I drove from Pella, through one the Macedonia wine roads 🛣️: such a scenic road through vineyard!

I spoke on the phone to a lovely young woman, Aggela, and we set up a visit on the spot!

It was such a lovely visit, so lovely! I met the man himself Kostis Dalamara.

An honest, knowledgeable family.

And, of course, tasting wine from one of the best, if not the best, in the wine 🍷 game in Greece 🔥.

I had such a great time at the winery! Thymiopoulos is a legend.

Nikos, pictured, organized the visit. Communication was so easy. The drive there scenic, and the selection of wines of course. It came with cold cuts and cheese.

Such warm hearted and kind people

What a classy bar! Run by classy people! 😉
Even the doorman was dressed classy, so classy I had to ask him if he was the doorman!

Shout out to Xristos the bartender for being do knowledgeable, courteous and kind. I had a Greece inspired Martini (Johnny's Greek I believe), a Saint Germain spirit and an aged Tsipouro.

I can't wait to return! 😍🔥🫴🙏🏿💚

Swanky bar with old school 90s vibe and a modern twist/vibe.

They have a zero waste philosophy. The menu is seasonal. They have a green 💚 house on the roof top that a lot of their botanicals come from.

The bar was suggested to me by the homies at Cercle de Salonique : shout out to Xristos!

Thanks to Angelos the bar tender and the rest of the crew for showing me looovveee! 🙏🏿🔥🫴

Lovely, lovely coffee shop and bar with warm service.

Found it by happenstance! Had an Americano and would love to come back for a drink!

Lucy is fantastic and such a warm person!

I had just gotten off a 36 hour trip. Meeting her, the difficulty of the travel melted away.

She went out of her way to accommodate my arrival schedule, and she is the manager! 🤯

I stayed in the spacious spacious Lycabetus sweet, which was spotlessly clean, with marble floors and decorated marvelously with Christmas fair. You can tell Lucy and Mets Suite pay attention to details and to the comfort of their guests.

Cleaning is every couple of days. Communication with her on WhatsApp was easy, seamless and straightforward.

Shout out to Contessa who kept the room spotless!

P.S.: Mets is my favorite part of Athens near the center! See my other reviews!

Come show love to chef Michael and his team!

Unfortunately, I was too busy enjoying my time to take photos. My friend and I had a fantastic time!

The decor of this place is super creative and amazing, classy but not pretentious, artsy. As a vinyl lover, I love the fact that they have a different DJ spinning every night. The acoustics of the place are amazing 🤩😍.

The wine selection is very nice! We had a bottle of Hatzidakis Assyrtiko. For food, everything we ate was amazing and of good size to us: tomato salad, mackerel with buttermilk, grilled grouper collar and the chef's twist on dolmadakias 😍.

We capped it off with the cheesecake 😊🧸🔥 and Hatzidakis vinsanto!

In Athens, with you, loves 💕😘.

Rainy afternoon ☔ in the neighborhood, needed a place to snack, sat down at this lovely place I've been meaning to try.

I was welcome with smiles and warmth.
The place has a lot of really good energy to it 😃😊.

I had a healthy snack as you can see, which was lovely 🤩 and very colorful 🙏🏿.

Everything tasted great: the creamy hummus, the crunchy on the outside, moist in the inside and we'll seasoned falafel 🧆, and the creamy couscous salad.

In Pangrati, Athens, with you, my loves 💕🙏🏿🌊.

Greeted and attended to by a polite young man grinding! It was August 14…. The next day is a BIG holiday. Athens is empty, these kids are grriinnddiing.

The place is super clean.

Simple, unassuming menu, that packs a punch 👊🏿 ✊🏿.

Dude was eating his own product too. You have to respect that!

I had their simplest smashed cheeseburgers. It's all in the meat: theirs is quality and has a flavorful, unique seasoning 🔥. The bun was soft and fluffy 😻, but not chewy as some of the cheap ones can get.

I washed the whole thing down with an ice cold coca cola 🥤 you dig 😉. It also looks like they have beer on tap, and that you can order a cocktail 🍸 too.

In Pangrati, Athens, with you my loves 💕🤫 💜.

P.S.: I visited a second time. First impressions confirmed!

I stumbled on this shop as I was looking for something to gift an office I was visiting. Chocolate was my first thought, but August in Greece 🙄.

The shop is extremely clean, well decorated and attended by a thoughtful man when I visited: George. He made an amazing bouquet following my guidance and his own personal touch.

In Athens, with you, loves 💕🎥🧸.

I've had the Fortune to travel widely and taste different kinds of meat.

I also had the Fortune to live in the neighborhood where Mr. Papoulias does business.

I've visited this store on three occasions. I have received such courtesy, true Greek philoxenia. On two occasions, I bought ribeye steaks: these are by faaarr the best Ribeyes I've tasted in my life: lean, soft, melts in your mouth, juicy and an almost herbal taste that probably comes from the diet of the animal.

The store is inviting, clean (very clean) and is staffed by thoughtful people. Shout out to Cimon! Mr. Papoulias himself, who I've seen every time I visited the store, looks like a stately individual.

Come support them!

In Athens, Pangrati, with you, my darlings 🙏❤️.

One of the top fish taverns I've been to.

Friendly service, without being overbearing. The right amount of attention for me. I sat inside, which probably helped to have a calm experience, as everyone else except another table was outside by the sea.

The decor is very Santorini. The music was traditional Greek island music.

I had the bogue 🥰😍, a cross between a white fish and a mackerel/sardine.

I had the breaded crayfish bites! Mississippi goddamn! 🤯

The xorta seasonal greens and the scordalia garlic dip were 🔥 too. In Santorini, they make it with bread. In other places I went it was with potatoes. Fire anyway, fire.

In Santorini, goddammit! With you, my loves 💕, with you ❣️😍.

Cool, peaceful accommodations, with a very calming vibe 😊. I thought the room I stayed in was decorated well. It has an unassuming charm to it.

I very much loved the wood theme, the earthy colors. The hotel is amazingly quiet and peaceful ! It is located in Perissa, towards the end of the town. From here, you're not far from Pyrgos, the highest village in Santorini, with a lot of cool bars, restaurants etc...andva night life that is more vibrant in my opinion than in Perissa. It was great to stay in Perissa as a quiet retreat and be able to go out to other towns for stimulation/activities.

Shout out to Manthos, the polite young man who welcomed me so thoughtfully and helped me to find a scooter 🛵. He was also very attentive during my stay. Shout to Sofia who made an amazing breakfast every morning!

I love traditional cooking.
I love creativity and playfulness.

This is hands down one of the top three places I've eaten in Greece that strikes a fantastic balance ♎⚖️ between the two.

The restaurant is located in Pyrgos, the highest village in Santorini. You get a fantastic view of the Aegean from their balcony. The establishment is decorated very well with nice earthy white and brown colors inside. The music is not too loud.

Spectacular establishment, simply lovely 🤩, especially because of the thoughtful and kind staff that works there. Shout out to Dimitri for being such an amazing host both times I visited.

I had a glass of Mavrotragano, a red local variety that survived the plague of the 1800s. I'm a big fan of Xinomavro. This is like its island, chill cousin 😎 and, perhaps, the best Greek red I've had.

I also had the lamb sausages! Unique!

On my second visit, I had the lamb leg in lemon sauce, artichokes and fennels 🤯🤩😍.
The lamb was soft and sweet. The lemon sauce tangy enough. And the fennel added a touch of herbal 😍🔥😎.

In Santorini, with you mes amours 😍🧡😻.

Shout out to the sommelier of Rita bar who sent me here!

Fantastic tour with a young oinologist Anna.
She was very thoughtful, knowledgeable. She did a fantastic job of telling me the story of the winery as well as their wine making process: vine cultivation, pressing, fermentation, bottling, testing in their cool lab!

Too much to share in a post like this!

It's a peaceful cellar.

It was started by Haridamos Hatzidakis and Konstantina Chrisou. They were the first to produce a 100% red from Mavrotragano.

I highly recommend you do the tour and learn about the family behind the operation. Anna's amazing story telling was very touching.

Two options: one hour and three hours.

Anna's pacing was great, both during the tour and during the tasting. She did a great job at giving me space during the tasting.

location.address: Epar.Od. Pirgou Kallistis - Profiti Ilia, Santorini 847 00, Greece
location.name: Rita Bar

properties.review_text_published:
Top notch, high class establishment w/o the pomp. A gem. Amazing views, of course. Amazing sunset. And, more importantly, a staff that is so thoughtful and with such great energy.

The place is decorated amazingly well, with earthy white colors and an avant guarde, Bohemian type of look. They played soothing house music 🎶 both times I went there.

Their sommelier is very knowledgeable and made me try new grape varieties I did not know about, such as Aidani. She suggested I visited Hatzidakis estate, which was also an amazing experience! 🙏🏿

I ate the stuffed octopus 🐙, pictured, which tasted amazing 😍: it's stuffed with rice 🍚 mixed with other shell fish. The lemon made it work really well.

Shout out to Pascal 🙏🏿

I'm Santorini with you, my loves 🙏🏿🥰♥️.

location.name: DIMITRIS Antiparos Rent A Car | Moto | Bike

properties.review_text_published:
Dimitri is a sweet, thoughtful person and a serious business man.

People like him and he likes people.

I rented a buggy one day and a scooter 🛵 another day. I had a great experience both times. I actually talked to him for a bit. He's been in the business for 30 years.

The buggy was fun, though I enjoyed the scooter 🛵 much more, especially on the windy day I rode!

He's your Guy here 🙏🏿

Woman owned, woman run, thoughtful,  accommodating service. Great baked products and other product selections.

Fantastic vibes all along.

Afros I was told means foam or froth, including the kind you see in the ocean 🌊.

In Antiparos, with you 🙏🏿🌊💕.

Not just good vibes, fantastic, peaceful vibes!

Stopped by for an afternoon drink after off-roading on the island. I was welcome at the bar and serenaded with great reggae music.

The space is expansive (spacious) 😝, decorated with an island feel.

So glad I found it.

In Antiparos, loves 💕, with you 🙏🏿.

I walked past this shop many times and never dared to enter until it was time to travel back home and I walked past again: it drew me in like a siren.

I wanted to buy vegan, eco friendly, natural, made in Greece products for my girl who is a beauty nut 🥜😅.

Dimitra who helped me was very kind, patient and thoughtful. I walked out with some beard oil for myself. At no point did I feel like I was being pressured. On the contrary. She was very gracious in handling my cluelessness at women's beauty practices and in giving me a woman's perspective.

The establishment is very inviting, well kept, clean and has a calming presence to it ☺️.

I definitely hope to pay them another visit soon.

In Athens, my loves, with you. Being Bougie as Bougie does 😅🙏🏿😎

Lovely little beach front restaurant where I had my first taste of Maldivan food! 😍

I order from the Lunch & Dinner Specials: I had the Rihaakuru Dhiya with rice 🍚, which I could lovely and flavorful. The portion size was the right amount for my level of hunger at the time: medium.

The fresh coconut water 🌊 💦 was lovely too.
They split it open for me in the end 🔚!

And the service: on point my guy, on point! ☝🏿
Shout out to Vijay, who pays attention to details like I have rarely seen! 🤯

The establishment is very clean 🫧 too and well kept.

The only thing I'll judge them for is their playlist 😅.
Joking, of course.

In the Maldives, with you, my darlings 🙏🏿😎🎒.

By far the best meal I had in Lakonia, paired with the best wine I had, too, from Tsimbidi winery—both one of their reds and one of their rosés.

I opted for a few small plates instead of a big meal.The fried cheese 🧀 from Mani was fantastic 😍: moist on the inside, really moist and crunchy on the outside. The split peas 🫛 with the truffle oil, scallions and sun-dried tomatoes was fire 🔥🚒 too 😱. I had the grilled meatballs with cumin, raisins, pistachios 🤤—really different from any meatballs I've tried elsewhere in Greece. I must say the best meatballs I have had have been in the Peloponnese.

In Peloponnesus, with you my loves 🫶🏿.

The atmosphere is nice 🙂. The music is Greek tunes with a World War 2/Belle Époque feel 🔥.

I am so grateful I found this place on my way to Marathon Beach! I was literally on the way, decided to look up places nearby where I could have a snack, and stumbled on this lovely taverna run by a father and son team.

It is hidden behind an amazing tree canopy. The atmosphere feels so welcoming and relaxing. The tree canopy very effectively shelters you from the sun. It's amazing. Despite the fact that the main road is nearby, it feels very peaceful ☺️

I had the paidakia, grilled lamb, a Greek salad, and fries 😍. Everything worked well—the lamb so moist, the ingredients in the salad so fresh, the olive oil 🛢️🤯.

Shout out to the owner, who makes his own wine—🔥 by the way—and his nephew for the φιλοξενία.

In Marathon, with you my loves 💕🙏🏿💪.

Come support the lovely people 😍 of Popolaros!

I've been living in this neighborhood for a while now and have patronized almost every establishment here. This is the latest!

I came by for Brunch on a lovely 😍🌹 Sunday morning in Athens and could not have been happier 😊.

They made me these lovely pancakes with bacon and eggs 🥚🥚🥚! Sweet, savory! The salad was a nice touch on the side, as was the the dill sprinkled on top!

Shout out to Eva and Maria! Eva's got great wine taste! One of her Rosés in particular! 🙏🏿

In Athens with you, my loves 💕.

I write this review with lots of pleasure and enthusiasm! 😃

After a lovely day at the beach ⛱️, 15 minutes nearby, my buddy and I stopped by this establishment for dinner and had a fantastic time 😍.

All other establishments on the time square were nearly full. We loved the fact that this one was tucked in, next to a lovely meadow, on a quiet evening with a lovely breeze.

I'm back baby. Back in Greece, my loves 💕.

We had the saganaki fried cheese 🧀: a lovely twist here is that the cut it up in squares. We had the seasonal greens, horta, the tomato and cucumber salad 🥗, a beet root salad, fried smelts and a lavraki whole fish! 🐟 I enjoyed everything 😊. It was fresh!

Come support these hard working people!

The cats are lovely. They didn't bother us too much and they seemed to be having fun! 😊

location.address: 955 Massachusetts Ave, Cambridge, MA 02139, United States
location.name: The UPS Store

properties.review_text_published:
Shout out to Gio and Jordan for their patience.

I went to print a book and have it staple bound. The book wasn't printing right. The shop was busy. It took us a few attempts to get it right.

Super happy with the result!

I've been living in this amazing neighborhood 😃 🤫for a while now. I finally decided to give this establishment a try. I showed up a bit before they were open, said hello and we're quite amicable, understanding and communicative about the fact that they were setting up! I appreciated that.

Nice, soothing rock was playing while I was waiting. Together with the wind and the dim light on this afternoon, this gave the joint an almost twilighty vibe.

I had the cheese burger 🍔, medium rare and a coke. Something simple. And it delivered. It's a big burger 🍔🤤. I particularly liked the cole slaw like salad they put on the burger.

Clean joint, warm service. Burger that delivers.

In Athens, with you αγάπες μου 🔥🐎😘.

location.address: 20 Rue de Claye, 77990 Le Mesnil-Amelot, France
location.name: L’ARGANIER Restaurant Le Mesnil Amelot

properties.review_text_published:
I had an overnight layover in Paris near CDG. I wanted couscous. I found this place and am glad!

I showed up 30 minutes before close and they were kind enough to sit and serve me.

The decor and ambiance are lovely: soft yellow, red and green. Soft music. A very clean space and a lovely host, Khaled. A true professional, who studied hospitality at a top school in Lausanne Switzerland, moved to France and has been in the game for 23 years. And it shows in his demeanor, in the look of the establishment.

The couscous tasted like it's made with heart, especially with the harissa sauce! The merguez are some of the best I've had anywhere.

I ended things with the "assiette gourmande": Moroccan tea with an assortment of Moroccan sweets!

Merci, Khaled! 😌 🙏🏿 🌍

Cute, presentable family run business.

A lot of the places around seemed packed and understaffed. This one seemed wide open!

I was greeted and seated by a warm and thoughtful seeming lady.

This place is clean, very clean and pays attention to detail. It's well organized with nice earthy blue 🔵, white 🤍 and brown 🟤 colors.

I was super hungry and my meal took a bit long to come out, but it was super worth it! The pork sirloin with rice pilaf.

I find it impressive that, in this place, you can eat a meal with very nice presentation, at a very reasonable price.

In Athens, with you 🙏🏿.

They're hard to get to on the phone 📱 last minute! 😅😜 But soo worth it!

As soon as I spoke to Giannis, a very warm and soft spoken man on the phone, I knew the drive from Athens would be worth it. The last leg, as you climb up to the vineyard is soothing and scenic as you arrive.

I was greeted by the sweetest and loveliest lady 😍, Frosso, who asked "did you come from Athens just for us?". When I answered "yes!", she said "thank you" 🙏🏿

The space is classy and elegant, very similar to Krista in Portaria that I reviewed. The building feels rustic, and the contrast of the brown 🟤 white 🤍 colors works well! They were playing soothing Jazz classics, Louis Armstrong, Ella Fitzgerald 💖... I just felt good being here man: the lovely lady sat me by a window, so I was able to peek outside at the vineyards from time to time...

I started with the bread and butter: some of the best homemade butter 🧈 I've tried anywhere, and definitely in Greece: smooth, creamy, with herbs 🌿. I also started with their skin contact white wine, the Vigneron Grec, which is, as far as I remember, Assyrtiko aged with Moschato skin: it's unfiltered and tasted tannic, acidic as I like it, and you could really feel the fruitiness.

My first dish was the mushroom and Feneos fava puree: look at the colors on this dish! 😜😍 The combination of textures on this dish is spectacular: the smooth fava puree, the al dente mushrooms, the crunchy small crumbs, and the chives! The winner of this show were definitely the mushrooms, definitely.

Next 🙄. We moved to the lamb garboudakia fricassee: garboudakia is like kokoretsi. It's an offal wrapped in intestines kind of sausage 😍. I was recommended to pair this with a white wine because of the greens: I went with their Assyrtiko because of the greens. This was the best, most aromatic Assyrtiko I've ever tried: what first hits you is the smell on the nose. I found it semi sweet, which worked well with the bitterness of the greens and the strong offal taste. The greens on this were fantastic and, afaik, this dish also has egg lemon sauce, avgolemono 🔥✨🐎🙈

Now. Big boys are gonna do what big boys are going to do. I had to have the dry aged lamb. Look at my review history 😂. This is the best piece of lamb 🐑 I've had in a while. I never thought you could age lamb 🙄. It felt like it was herb 🌿 infused. The potatoes were amazing as well. Why me?! 😭: look at the colors on this dish! I spend a lot of time in the kitchen, the most surprising thing about this dish was the bell pepper 🌶️ tapenade (Greek bell peppers, technically, from Florina). It worked and I would not have thought 🤔 about it.

Now, for the surprise: Mavro Styfo, a rare Greek grape 🍇 you will, likely only get to taste here: tannic, off the chart 📈 aromas on the nose, not full bodied but just enough to work with the lamb.

Et maintenant, pour la fin: chocolate mousse! Texture, texture, texture. Flavor flavor flavor! Contrasts! Smooth, crunchy (caramelized macadamia nuts and pistachios?), the olive 🫒 oil! Not too sweet 🎂, slightly salty! 💋 😍

Giannis knows his craft. A true, no bullshit professional. Woorrdd. And a great conversationalist. I was having great food and getting an education at the same time.

I had a chic bougie 😊 shopping experience here, with one of the lady owners playing dress up with me 😅. Shout out to them for making me feel like a million bucks trying all kinds of fire 🔥 silk scarves that fit me well! I could have left with the whole store.

It was one of the most thoughtful shopping experiences I've had! The ladies took the time to explain to me the differences between different silk tissages (knitting methods). They also took the time to give me lessons on how to fold scarves 🔥. Listen, that soft silk feeling: I could have slept there 🙄😅😍.

Overall, a thoughtful, educational and fun time.

Shout out to Madame Vanessa, Lucinda and Flora! Thank you for welcoming me to your space!

À bientôt 🙏🏿

A very peaceful place that was recommended by a local friend as a suitable one to spend a few hours working from. The place has a calming presence to it. From the earthy blue grey, to the art deco/Scandinavian design space feel, to the soothing music that is not loud: when I walked in at 9 am or so, just a narrative soundtrack and soothing bossa music were playing. A space that feels good to be in.

The best Americano I've had: I've found it challenging in Greece to have an Americano just at the right temperature. It's usually too cold or too hot. This was just right and served in an earthen cup🍵. Smooth, no sugar, just bitter enough 😋🙏🏿.

I had the ginger lemonade 🍋. I enjoyed it, though I must admit I would have preferred it less sweet. It was beautifully served.

The savory pancakes 🥞 were 🔥: a cheesy, bechamel like sauce on top, poached egg, bacon 🥓, and importantly to me, not too sweet! 💟😋.

The place features a separate space with jewelry, crafts etc...

Overall, a warm establishment that pays lots of attention to detail! Kudos to them for building such a welcoming space, and shout out to the young lady that attended me for her thoughtfulness 🙏🏿.

In Athens, with you my loves 💕😚🙏🏿.

Peaceful, clean burger joint with an old school Tarantino flick style/feel to it and simple offerings. The locale is super clean, bright and is decorated with red and white tiles. Charming and cute 🥰 is the vibe I got ☺️.
The music 🎶 is not loud at all and is very soothing.

I got a chance to speak with Arlo and Ismini who run this joint and they were amazingly thoughtful and kind. They explained to me that their concept is zero waste! Everything they sell is consumed and not wasted, hence no fries🍟, vegetables etc...

I had the double patty burger. It is the best I've had in Athens. The meat 🍖 is juicy, the bun soft and the sauce 🔥, as in super flavorful. I could definitely taste the mustard. I'm unashamed to say that I went for a second burger ρε, φίλε 🙄🤣. Εντάξει.

Overall, a cute joint with simple, tasty offerings, and an educational experience. They do one thing and do it well!

In Athens with You 😘🙏🏿.

I found out about this show through banners in the street while in Volos. I was able to get an electronic ticket 🎫 online, which you have to present when you arrive at the venue, so they can turn it into a paper ticket.

I enjoyed the show, particular the breadth of the performances. If you are a fan 😎 of acrobatics, dancing and stunts, you'll find something for yourself. Both adults and children seemed to be enjoying themselves.

location.name: The Train of Pilio (Ano Lekhonia - Milies)

properties.review_text_published:
My AI 🙄 suggested I check this out while in Volos! 😅It's a little known train 🚂 ride through the Pilio mountain 🏔️ from Ano Lechonia to Milies.

As far as I understand, during the season, the train runs once out of Ano Lechonia (around 10 00) to Miles (around 15 00) and back, on Saturday and Sunday. You can buy tickets online.

It's a fun ride in my opinion, especially if you do just stay inside the train but also venture to the small balcony at the back of the train! 🚃

Come to Pilio, habibi 😘 😍.
Just keep it on the low low 🤫.

Because I did not intend to spend time in Milies (did that the previous day), I arranged for a taxi to drive me back to Ano Lechonia so I could get back to my car.

I came by this joint, Barracuda, twice. The first time, the kitchen was already closed, despite the internet saying otherwise 🙄. I'm glad I gave them a second chance.

It's a small, festive joint with lots of character. That's why/how they got me! I love their choice of colors. That blue turquoise gets me every time. Both the inside 💠 and the outside were lovely/lively. As you can tell from the video, there was a live band playing pop classics. The young woman singing had a beeauutiful voice 😍🙏🏿. It just feels like a happy, festive, bubbly 💭 place 😊. The young man who greeted me the first night 🌃 welcomed me again the second, recognized me and welcomed me warmly.

It's a tsipouradiko (see my review of Rada). You get Mezes when you order tsipouro 🙄. I got fried baby shrimp 🦐 🍤 and touramasalata: I love ❤️ how they make it in Volos...creeaammyyy 🤤😋🥰.

Habibi, if you think you've tried touramasalata, come to Volos 😊🥰🙏🏿.

Flavia, Sofia and their team are what you call true professionals. A one in a kind team! 😍😊

Communication with them is easy. Despite being a low maintenance guest, I loved their availability on WhatsApp.

I stayed at one of their apartments on two separate occasions.

The first time, my plane was delayed by 6+ hours! Sofia, bless her heart, was there at the apartment to meet me! After giving the run down, I was settled within an hour.

The second time was on Good Friday, an important day for Orthodox Greeks. They still said they would meet me to hand off keys 🙏🏿.

The apartment itself: clean, spacious, gorgeous 🥰, with attention to detail and a beautiful view of almost half of Athens.

It is located in a vibrant neighborhood called Mets, as almost every Greek who I told where I live concurred. You're at, in my opinion, an optimal 20-minute walk from the center so that you can get all that energy when you want, or stay around Mets/Pagrati and get a hip village in the city vibe.

A real Acropolis. Bare bones. Simple. Impregnable. I've been to the Acropolis in Mykines, two of them. This was by far the rawest, most rewarding one.

The climb is is not for the weak. The breath taking views. The sound of the wind 🌬️. Eagles 🪽 flying, in suspension, surfing the wind, hunting snakes 🤯. The sound of silence. There is nothing to steal. No fancy Parthenon. Nothing. What will you take? Go ahead. For you philosophy buffs, who think about the Acropolis of the mind, this place will speak to you...

properties.review_text_published:
My friend, who I took to an African restaurant for the first time, called this a small paradise in Istanbul 😍. And yes, indeed. As you may read on my profile, when I review, I only leave 5 star reviews. This is one of the two I left in Istanbul. The other is for Bosphorus spa.

The space is so clean, so clean. The choice of warm blue 🔵, white 🤍 and other soft colors gives it a very calming presence 😊💟. The choice of music was African of course, very nice upbeat African pop.

We were attended to by Knife, a young, artistic Ivorian woman studying as a film maker in Istanbul. Shout out to her, and shout out to Mr. Amadou Diallo, one of the other people working here.

We shared the fish 🐟 pastels, which were fire, the tieb with fish (Senegal's national dish) and the lamb dibi. All were tasty and well seasoned in the way you'd expect West African food to be! 😏.

The menu features a mix of popular West African dishes. The food is tasty 😋/flavorful, and the ambiance festive 🙏🏿🌍✊🏿.

location.name: Bosphorus Spa

properties.review_text_published:
My friend and I had a fantastic time at this establishment. We started communicating with the owner Cetin on WhatsApp who, right off the bat, was very warm thoughtful, accommodating and candid. This was a breath of fresh air for what had been a challenging experience up to that point in Istanbul.

We had the amazing 1.5 hour combo. The ladies were super warm and came out feeling brand new.

Get this. My friend forgot her phone and we were able to recover it easily (selfie) He even gave us a ride to our next destination.

Thank you, Çetin! 😍🙏🏿😃

Nikos the bartender is the man! A civil engineer by trade who found his calling in bartending. And it shows : drinking here with him was like being taken in a journey by such a soft spoken and humble person. I came to this bar two nights in a row while in Volos! It's in a street lined with other bars. The vibe just spoke to me. I sat down at the bar and Nikos seemed like such a warm and thoughtful dude.

The place just has a great festive vibe to it! With lots of beautiful 😻 people, Volos style. Dig. On both occasions, Friday and Saturday night 🌉, there was a DJ playing (Afro) House music 🎵🎶. Ok, the music was a bit loud at the bar, but hey... it's a bar on weekend nights...The lighting is dim, which gives it a speak easy vibe. They have plenty of tables inside and outside if you want a sit down kind of vibe. From what I could see, they also serve bar kinda food. I saw a few burgers floating around. The place just feels put together man,  so to say. On both nights there was a person who looks like their job, like an eagle 🦅, was to survey the place and make sure everything is smooth ☺️.

I tried many cocktails: The Traveler, 50/50 Margarita, Green 💚 Tonic, the Pearhattan, a Penicillin and a Mezcal Negrori. All hits to me, especially number 1 (gingeryyy) and 4. I liked that their cocktails weren't too sweet🍸.

Shout out to Giannis, too! The man with the backwards baseball cap!

In Volos, with YOU 😋🥰🙏🏿.

I spent a night a this hotel 🏨 as I needed a place to crash before flying out of Athens for a few days. I also needed a place that would store bags 🛍️ for me while I was away.

I called it advanced to inquire about my bags and talked to a very soft spoken lady, Fotini, who reassured me they'd be happy 😊 to store my bags! 😍 Amazing! This took care of one of my biggest concerns.

Check in was smooth and a breeze!

The pictures are from their restaurant, Forky's where I had breakfast.

Overall, Artemida, the town where the hotel with is a small sea side town where Greek families come to summer ⛱️ vacation.

Shout out to the young lady who stores my bags 🛍️ for me! And to the young man and woman who offered to help with my bags when I arrived! 😀

Cute, peaceful space. I met the owner, Athena, the night before at another bar, an underground bar.

The best way I can describe this place is a thoughtful space, really a reflection of the owner. It's on a cute narrow street. The smallest, most intimate bar in Volos in my opinion. The music 🎵🎶 is not loud at all. Athena is a musician and very musically experienced and literate. She plays some rock and entertained us with African and world 🌍 music when I was there. She paints too, and her walls are lined with some of her creations.

I came to Volos to drink tsipouro. I always wondered why I could not find a cocktail 🍸 with tsipouro anywhere. Athena has got two: Rakito and Rakita, mojito/margarita with Raki! I tried the Rakita! It was delicious 😋.

As Athena put it, her concept was to build a place in Volos where you would feel that you are part of the world.

She succeeded in my opinion.

Come to Volos, habibi 😘🙏🏿☺️.

This establishment was across the street from my hotel 🏨 in Volos! I patronized them three times! On the final day, I decided to give their pastries a try!
The apple 🍎🍏 pie 🥧, fire baby 🐥, fire 🔥.

Their space is super inviting, and they offer a range of products including wine, pastries, dried fruit, smoothies etc...It seems run by a group of young 🌱, soft spoken and thoughtful women.

In Volos, habibi! Come! 🔥🌱😊.

This beautiful establishment is right at the center of town on a cute cobble street, right in front of a street lined with popular establishments (coffee shops, bars etc...) and surrounded by other great spots to eat.

Communication was super easy. The check in instructions were clear and easy to follow.

The apartments themselves are spacious, clean and welcoming, with warm lighting, as is the building itself. You can tell they lay attention to details!

At the last minute, I extended my stay by a night and had to change rooms. They were kind, accommodating and very responsive to text messages.

Believe the reviews!

This place was recommended to me by a gentleman I met at La Nonna in Mets, who I told about my trip to Volos.

The next morning after I arrived, I decided to call and schedule a tour. I got Apostolia on the phone who was very patient, soft spoken and just exudes great energy, great energy!

God bless her heart 💗, I called again to confirm and also change my plans slightly because, you know, stuff happens and she was super happy to accommodate. I initially booked a three hour ride with another group! I ended up taking a two hour ride in the forest with her and another young lady.

Meeting her in person. She and her space have such a calming effect and presence. As do her horses, her dogs and cats. I hadn't ridden in a while. She did a great job at making me feel relaxed.

We took a ride through the mountains, visited an ancient monastery connected to the Greek War of Independence and Milies, Άγιο Νικόλαο I think. She did a great job narrating the story of the monastery. On the way back, we got an impromptu botany lesson: she would stop now and then, point out plants and herbs to us, especially since both myself and the other person on the tour love food! ❤️

In Πήλιο with you, my loves 🐎😍🙏🏿

Amazing, amazing handmade and traditional jewelry store in Volos! I was walking around, shopping for a gift 🎁 for a friend. I asked around a few stores selling traditional, handmade jewelry, and found my DeTales 😍.

The store was closed the Saturday I came and, lucky me, it was open the day after, which is not usually the case!

The beautiful and kind woman running the shop, Nikki, was so patient with me as I was browsing for my gift. She listened carefully, was very soft-spoken and at no point pressured me. This was super important for me especially as I like to take my time browsing! ☺️

You can tell that love goes into the jewelry she sells, lots of love.

I walked out with a quartz/silver/abalone necklace for my friend, and a rustic silver bracelet for myself! 😉😁

I walked 10-15 minutes from the center to this place! And I'm so glad I did. So glad! It is a family-run establishment. I was greeted by a lovely woman and a man who used to be a naval officer. Lovely and friendly people! Another "highly" rated place I tried going to was closed despite their hours advertising otherwise.

It's a Tsipouradiko that only serves fish: the concept is that for every bottle of Tsipouro you order, you get a few Mezes. I ordered two bottles and got three: the best taramasalata I've had anywhere, so creamy and buttery, the black-eyed peas with smoked fish pastirma and grilled mackerel with potatoes 🥰😋💕. Despite the simplicity of how it all reads and looks, it's hard to describe how fresh and unique it all tasted: the sea in your mouth! 👄
Greek Meze culture at its finest: I never truly understood the concept of a Meze. Now I think I do.

The aesthetics of the place itself gives the feel of a fishing lodge: it totally makes sense that the husband was a marine officer 😄 😉. The stone walls, the decoration, the wooden grey and white scaffolding. The music 🎶 is a combination of traditional and modern pop Greek 🇬🇷 music, at just the right volume.

The poem in the picture translates to:

"Τη νύχτα σου 'πα στο καμπούνι μια ιστορία την ίδια που όλοι οι ναυτικοί λένε στη ράδα τα μάτια σου τα κυβερνούσε σοροκάδα, κι όλο μουρμούριζες βραχνά:

Φάλτσο η πορεία..."

Νίκος Καββαδίας

"At night I told you in the cabin the same story that all sailors tell in the roadstead, your eyes were ruled by the sirocco wind, and you kept muttering hoarsely:

The course is wrong..."

Nikos Kavvadias

Ράδα (Rada) means "roadstead" (a sheltered area near shore where ships can anchor).

When it came time to pay, I couldn't believe it all only cost me 10 euros. Good, honest people. I can't wait to come back.

In Volos, Greece with you, my loves 💕.

I stopped by this lovely spot on the way to Farsalus from Volos. It's a no frills joint serving gyros (pork, chicken) and other grilled meats/sausages.

They looked busy behind the counter! Their phone 📱 was blowing up too, constantly ringing. A lovely young lady, Christina, was patient enough to tell me what was grilling.

I decided for the pork gyro, but not with the regular gyro meat: with the roasted pork on the spit 🤤😍. The young lady suggested this! With a side of fries, which were to die for!

The sandwich 🥪 itself was easy to eat and not messy at all!

The young lady wanted to know how to say "Bon appétit" in English: "Bon appétit"! 😅😂

In Volos Greece, with you my loves 💕.

This place is just fantastic and I would describe it as quite a multi-sensory experience. I stayed in Volos. This is in the mountains. I called the night before and was greeted by a patient and lovely man, who told me their hours and reassured me I did not need a reservation but made one anyway. Their concept is truly unique and remarkable: true farm-to-table (they own their farm), consume locally sourced products, eat seasonally. Ask them about their sister restaurants/cafés!

First of all, the drive up from Volos is just amazing and breathtaking, especially as you climb closer to Portaria.

To me, this restaurant feels like a multi-sensory experience! 💯 It feels like your classy friend's living room, with the fancy kitchen nearby, nice smells and aromas and the whole vibe. When I walked in, as someone into deep aromas and odors, the first thing that hit me is the lovely, subtle smell of ❤️‍🔥 burning wood from the chimney and food aromas that tease your stomach into submission! Pure pleasure, pure pleasure! Visually, the interior has the feel of a winter chalet. The music 🎶 comprises Jazz piano standards and is sooo soothing, contributing to an amazing 😍 experience.

I had the Trachana soup, the beet salad with cheese/yogurt, pickled onions and pistachios, and the lamb fricassee.

The soup is soo beautiful! It is a mixture of curdy, slightly sour, and well seasoned: it has feta-like cheese, a sausage out of this world...🌎 One of the top two soups I've had in Greece. The beet salad! 🥗 Mann, what can I say? For all of y'all who think beets are bland 🙄, you've never had beets like these before: the perfect mix of vinegary and sweet. The pistachios and the soft cheese add an amazing touch...

Finally, your boy ending things with the lamb with greens (Xorta) dish and egg lemon sauce! (Avgolemono). Even before tasting, the beautiful smell of dill and other herbs hit me in the face like a mule! 😅 This is the best, most original, stewed lamb 🐑 dish I've had in Greece: and I've been coming here for almost a decade.

To close things off, the apricot pie/tart 🙄🥧. Come try it. Nothing to say.

In Volos, αγάπη μου, with YOU 🥰🙏🏿🚀.

First night in Volos! The capital of Tsipouro and Mezedes. After a four-hour drive and settling at the hotel, I decided to feed the beast at this establishment near the hotel. It was quiet on a Tuesday night. This is not Athens. It's a small town. I didn't mind having a tasty meal without a crowd and without music in the background.

The outside of the place is very welcoming, with the warm light blue 🩵 color combined with yellow 🟡 and orange 🧡.

The menu features both regular plates, appetizers, as any taverna would, but also Mezes, which are a very Volos thing!

I decided to keep it simple and try simple: I had the omelette with pastrami and potatoes 😍🔥, and the eggplant soufflé with cheeses and bacon. Both simple and 🔥, well seasoned, and with tasty and quality ingredients. The eggplant melts in your mouth. The pastrami is salty enough but not overwhelmingly so.

Shout out to the lovely man, Christos, who fed a hungry traveler. Dude is impressive. He both took my order and made the food! Impressive.

In Volos, my loves 💕, with YOU 😋🥰🙏🏿.

Deli meats chocolate parlor meets wine/liquor store, meets awesome ness ☺️.

The space is very welcoming and cute! The lighting is particularly well picked in my opinion, and this particularly shines around the wine area. The music, too, is very soothing and adds a chic ambiance to the establishment.

The wine selection, btw, is amazing 😍! And the manager Katerina, who was very warm, patient, welcoming, and thoughtful, knows tons about wine! Tons! Greek wine especially!

I walked away with some nut bars, cookies and a wine bottle.

They seem to put a great emphasis on health, and so a lot of their sweets are sugarless!

Given the breadth and wealth of the products they offer, I was amazed at Katerina's deep knowledge about each of them. A true professional!

They also have coffee and fresh squeezed orange juice. You won't regret paying them a visit.

In Athens, with You 🥰🙏🏿.

Amazing bar and live traditional great music 🎶 joint Kakithea. My friend and I met the manager at a restaurant in Petralona. As a fan of traditional Greek music, I am so glad and thankful I found Kouros 😍🙏🏿.

I was treated so well and welcomed with open arms.

As some posts have warned, this place is NOT for the weak 😅😂🤣.

I look forward to returning.

In Athens my loves, with YOU 🥰🙏🏿.

This place stands out visually so much on the block it's on, might I say even on the whole very long avenue! 😍 The door front looks luxurious, with warm, brown, green and gold colors. The interior, although it looks small, is very expansive with art deco lamps in the ceiling.

The attention to detail and cleanliness makes this place remarkable in my opinion.

Everything is handmade, with natural products!
They sell the designer chocolates by the box; you can tell the price range from one of the photos. As you can also tell, there are many flavors! I bought a small box, tried one of them on the spot and maannn, one with pecan and praliné that is all black 🖤 with gold spots 🪙 : pure pleasure. I also love the fact that they are bite sized and not too big.

They also sell truffles, of which I tried one (hazelnut with dark 🌑 chocolate on the outside)…winning!

The dark chocolate hazelnut bar I had was fire 🔥 too.

Aside from taste, especially if you're a dude/msn who's not that much into sweets 🙄😂, I really appreciateed that the products did not leave that heavy feeling you get from cheap sweets. On second thought, you'd appreciate that as a woman ♀️ 👠 too. Nvmnd me 🤦🏿‍♂️.
The ingredients really feel top notch!

You will also find an assortment of chocolate bars, hazelnut spread and a bunch of other cute things like Easter eggs!🥚

Last but not least, I met the man himself (Peter) Panos, a soft spoken, thoughtful, humble dude, 😎 almost to a fault who knows his craft. I would not have known this is the owner if I had not asked. In fact, I rank this as one of the most thoughtful shopping experiences I've had, on par with some of the ones in Paris I wrote about recently: interacting with true artisans really does hit different and make you enjoy their products in a unique way!

Overall, I felt like shopping for sweets in Paris' Saint Germain des Prés!

In Athens with You 😘🙏🏿.

The place was packed on a cool, just right weather evening at 9 pm: thank you Google for saying "Not busy" 🙄😅.

Warm welcome 🤗 by a nice, soft spoken gentleman. I was told there'd be a wait for outside seating. Fine.

The place is painted with warm, earthy 🌍 colors. The light is dim to medium. Inviting, to me. The side street is lined with orange/citrus 🍋🍊 trees. On the Spring evening I came, the breeze spread an amazing orange blossom scent around 😍. Pure pleasure to the nose!

I don't usually wait 🙄, but their warmth, and their ability to communicate really won me over. Once I sat down, the person that attended me was super receptive to my ability to order quick! 😂

I decided, and I'd suggest, you try items you can't easily find anywhere else: I went for the nasitiko cheese 🧀, a funky graviera/gruyère like cheese, the shrimp skewers which seemed to be one of their specialties, and the marinated mullet! 😍

Outside a pumpkin aged, Roquefort like Greek cheese I tried somewhere else, this is one of the best, most different cheeses I've had in any taverna.

Even before I tasted them, the skewers smelled so nice. They were grilled to perfection for my taste, not too dry, not too moist, not too cooked.

Onto the marinated mullet. Not quite sure what I expected. A bit more chewy than I expected though. It was good, not wow. Next time, I'll likely go for the smoked mackerel.

This skewer 🍢🍡 joint was recommended to by a local local acquaintance when I told them I live in Mets. It's a no frill joint. Next door is a bar that looks quite popular.

I tried the chicken skewer (it's chicken thigh meat, my favorite! 🤤; very moist and less dry than breast imo), as well as the pork skewer and the kebab. Both also tasted great. The pork wasn't too dry. The kebab was fire 🔥. It was cooked medium and was moist inside.

Overall, a nice spot for a quick skewer stop.

I've eaten at this peaceful and welcoming establishment in Pagrati twice! Once with a friend, the other time by myself. Both times, super super service, warmth and thoughtfulness 💖🙏🏿😊.

As you can tell, the choice of colors is dark green and brown: the place has a bistro meets taverna feel, with belle époque inspired music playing at lunch the second time I visited. The place is on a corner with extensive outdoor seating and few tables indoors. Together, this gives it an expansive feel.

My first time, I had tasty chicken 🐔 and meat skewers with fries and a nice tomato + lettuce salad with a tasty tangy dressing.

The second time, I had a savory pie with feta and leeks, as well as the sausage from Crete. The pie was amazing: the dough moist, but not chewy, the filling well seasoned and tasty.

I really enjoyed the sausage: it had a barbecue taste to it, was chunky on the inside. It worked well with the lemon and the onions, really well!

This is an amazing brunch spot in Mets! I found out about it through someone I met at La Nonna across the street that works here. The patio is nice and the pistachio 💚 colors very warm.

Service. Service. Service! It's amazing here, and at a lot of the places around. I've visited twice and both times I was treated amazingly by the ladies working at the time. They were patient, attentive and far from overbearing.

The first time, I had their granola bowl, which was huge and layered with flavor. The candied grapes and lavender 🪻 flowers stood out.

The second time, I had the veggie omelette, with a side of bacon. The omelette was cooked to perfection for my taste: moist, gooey inside 😍 🤪.

Both times, I had a fresh squeezed orange 🍊 juice 🧃 and an Americano.

As the name suggests, this warm establishment is located on the side of a hill, in Metz, one of my favorite neighborhoods in Athens. I've reviewed a few other places here. It features a small interior with a bar, a free old school arcade 😍🤩 and, the highlight, a huge patio/outdoor seating area, that always seems busy. The interior and exterior are decorated with a soothing combination of earthy green 💚 and yellow 💛.

I come here often and have always been welcomed with a smile and thoughtfully, the most recent time by the owner Spiros who, seeing me often, took the time to say hello and inquire if I lived in the neighborhood. The fact that locals love this place is a great testament to its standing.

On my last visit, I tried the food. I had the fried egg, bacon and fries bowl. The order initially came without bacon 🥓. We communicated well and they offered to replace the whole order. The eggs were over easy and moist. The fries crispy. Bar-like food. A nice snack 😋.

I highly suggest you try their fresh squeezed orange 🍊 juice! 🧡

P.S.: I walked past on a Friday night, the bar area seemed packed with people dancing to a DJ's tunes and having a good time! 🙏🏿

location.address: 13 Rue du Cardinal Lemoine, 75005 Paris, France
location.name: Le Thiossane

properties.review_text_published:
I was on my way to find couscous in Île Saint-Louis! I've walked by Thiossane before but they were closed. This time, they were open so I decided to take a chance 🎲.

The place is beautifully decorated with African art and paintings lining the wall: it oozes a certain kind of Senegalese calm and Teranga. There was no music playing, which was nice and didn't feel awkward.

I had the Senegalese national dish (I grew up eating this): thiebou dieun rouge, a dish in the UNESCO World Heritage! It's rice cooked in a fish stew, with vegetables. As expected from Senegalese cuisine, the dish was flavorful and well-seasoned. The vegetables, in particular, came with a nice red sauce (matiaat) that really makes it come together. The vegetables include manioc, carrots, okra, aubergine, cauliflower.

As the place filled up, I could see that other people's dishes also had a very nice presentation, similar to mine.

P.S.: "Thiossane" in Wolof roughly means nostalgia for good old times.

As a drink, I'd recommend you try the Senegalese fruit juices if you haven't before: bissap (hibiscus), bouye (baobab fruit), ditakh (very unique, look it up 😅, rare to find outside Senegal).

How do I know the place is legit? 🤔 We Africans are hard to please when it comes to food from our countries: by the time I finished eating, more than half the people sitting were from Senegal. I had the ginger, with orange blossom water, ananas it seems, water and sugar. It's hot/spicy 🔥! Watch out! Pro tip: order the bissap and mix it with some ginger to take the edge 📴.

This establishment is in the cute Mets neighborhood of Athens. See my review of Olympion nearby to read more about the neighborhood.

The space is super welcoming: it has that Mediterranean, checkered garden table feel. It is lit mainly with draped/checkered lamps, which gives a speakeasy romantic kind of vibe, so a bit of romantic and edgy at the same time. The music is soothing and not overbearing. I love their choice of colors too: a mix of salmon, red, white, and brown. I very much enjoyed spending time at their bar the first time: I found it elegant with an edge. The people working here are very thoughtful. I'd say that this is a great place both for couples, friends looking to catch up, families, and other occasions.

The first time, my friend and I had the truffle pizza (white) and pepper pizza. Both were relatively big! 🤯😊. The dough felt just right to me, chewy with a slightly carbonized feel. The ingredients felt super fresh, especially on the truffle pizza 🍕: pure taste.

We also had the burrata, which came with an amazing honey dressing.

They have a nice wine list that includes Italian selections. I tried the Moschofilero and the Kir Yanni Xinomavro blend. Both tasted really nice!

The second time, I had the La Nonna Peinirli (pictured). First time I tried such a thing. Thin dough, moist egg 🥚, peppery 😋, stringy cheese. Niiiiceeee. The egg 🥚, wow, makes it really come together.

Before I left the second time, I moved to the bar and had a great conversation with one of the bartenders, Dimitri. He made me a great Mezcal margarita!

Overall, all flavor, thoughtfulness, and lovely vibes.

Walked past for an impromptu dessert after dinner nearby! There was a line outside at 19 30! It moved fast!

To my question of "éclair or tart", the nice lady behind the counter replied both! 😅🤣

The interior/exterior, with the mix of dark blue 💙 wood/tiles and white 🤍, is nice and bright!

Edit: the éclair au chocolat I had was magnificent! 😋
Light, fluffy outside. Creamy but not heavy filling. Most importantly to me, not too sweet at all.

location.address: 16 Rue des Fossés Saint-Jacques, 75005 Paris, France
location.name: BOULANGERIE MODERNE

properties.review_text_published:
I visited this place three or four times 😄.

Each time, I tried one of the classics: croissant 🥐, pain au chocolat 🍫, pain au raisin. Each time I was more than satisfied. I grew up on these things. They are the real deal. They taste like the real thing. I also tried an 🧅 tart, which was 🔥. And this is just the tip of all they have to offer, as you can tell from the pictures.

It seems like a popular spot among travelers so, depending on when you visit, you may have to wrestle your way, in front, not inside, through people taking photos etc...

It also seems like a neighborhood place: on one of my visits, I saw a guy, who looked like he works at an establishment nearby, pick up baguettes 🥖 multiple times. I saw a bunch of construction workers, too, stop by for breakfast.

On the coffee front, nothing fancy, just the basics from a coffee machine. I didn't mind my Americano that way.

Overall, a no nonsense, 🔥 boulangerie pâtisserie, popular among travelers and locals alike.

I walked in at 19:30 on a Saturday evening without a reservation. They let me know they were fully booked by 20:30 but could accommodate me if I took a reasonable amount of time. I told them I didn't want to rush. They said they understood. We communicated well and agreed to give it a chance.

It's a classy, rustic interior, as you can tell, with velvet red divan seats lining the wall. It has an old school meets modern sort of charm. The black and white photos lining the wall work well with the red and brown interior.

Downstairs is a space/bar you can rent for private parties 🎉/concerts.

The evening I came, dinner was going to be accompanied later by a live pianist 🎹

I had the classic duck confit with potatoes 🍠 roasted in the duck grease 😋😄. Who's living like a doctor?

The potatoes were perfect: moist yet not too soft. The duck too was moist and, surprisingly for the skin, worked well! Hidden under the duck, mixed with the potatoes were tiny chopped mushrooms 🍄 that added character and texture to the whole experience.

I came in and meant business, to eat and not linger. Overall, a great experience: I was fed well, given courtesy I don't take for granted.

I ended things with a glass of Champagne 🍾🥂 at the comptoir/bar by the entrance. As I hung out around there, I got to catch the team interacting with each other and being goofy 😜: they're a fun bunch. We chatted a bit. By the time I got warmed up and comfortable, the piano player started serenading the whole place with her sweet voice.

Shout out to Antoine and Alexandre at the bar! The marble heads lined at the top are from famous people that used to play music here back in the day 🔥🕯️🙏🏿.

I had an amazing shopping experience here! I learned about Acqua di Parma after staying at a nice hotel in Greece! I must admit they have an amazing selection of colognes, lotions and other body products!

Today, I was shopping for body lotion: this is the best lotion 🧴 I've tried all my life: hands down. For men especially, it almost feels like you haven't put any lotion on! The products are so natural that you are not left with a greasy feeling 🙄.

The lady who assisted me took the time to walk me through different products in their line. I almost walked out with a cologne too, but I'm a parfum guy !

Don't sleep on Acqua. Give them a try!

And oh, to top off the experience, the lady sweetly asked me "monsieur, est ce que je peux vous parfumer?"/"sir, can I put some of our cologne on you?" 🥳😅, to thank me I suppose! 🙏🏿

Merci!

location.address: 17 Bis Rue Campagne Première, 75014 Paris, France
location.name: Icosium Restaurant Algérien

properties.review_text_published:
I stayed in the 5e and tried going to a couscous joint multiple times: the place was closed. I found this joint in Google and decided to give them a call. I was greeted by a very soft spoken and warm man, Said, who, to my question as to whether they have couscous, answered "I have couscous every day!"

Jackpot! 🎰

The neighborhood that the establishment is in does not do the inside justice! It's a rustic, brown interior, exposed brick/rock walls and a blend of modern and Oriental feel. The place has a soft, soothing feel to it and is very clean. The lighting is warm and the music soft/not overbearing.

There were only a few (4 other) people when I walked in at 13:00. By the time I sat down, took it all in and received my meal, the place was half full.

Attention to detail characterizes this place: the bowl I ate my couscous in came warm. The harissa hot sauce 🔥 is traditional and tasty!

The best way I can describe this couscous, overall, is as "parfumée" (exuding beautiful scent). The legumes, the semoule, the smoky grilled veal/lamb and sausage. For a super taster such as myself, this was quite an olfactory experience. The lamb, the veal. The merguez. It's my first couscous with *grilled* meat and they were grilled medium rare, perfect for my taste.

Said is a very courteous and polite man who checked in from time to time without being overbearing. He seemed to get along very well, too, with other tables.

For your bougie 🙄😅 wine drinkers. Try the Algerian wine. Try the Algerian wine 🍷🙄🤙🏿🙏🏿.

I was standing on the corner at a spot nearby and saw a couple of young ladies with tasty looking sandwiches in their hands. They pointed me to this joint.

A literal hole in the wall.

Dig this. They only take cash. I didn't have any on me 😅. The two sweet ladies running the shop let me go in confidence that I'd come back and pay.

We also had a lovely chat about Africa, Vietnam, the US. They even made suggestions of authentic places to eat Vietnamese food in Paris.

As for the sandwich itself: lemongrass chicken, mayo, cucumber, cilantro, carrots, a hint of soy sauce. It hits different than any banh mi you'll have in the states, the bread, the taste, the experience with the ladies. You could really taste the lemon grass, the mayo and the layers of flavor and texture 🤤😋

Standing in the street enjoying it. I felt alive.

Merci, Madame Marie 😍🥰😘

I had a lovely Nutella crepe and an espresso here.

Walked in to three, four bubbly and welcoming ladies working here. Shout out to Sonan! I like my coffee hot hot hot 🔥 and, admittedly, was in diva mode 😅. They said the machine controls the temperature. I jokingly insisted and asked them to talk to the machine!

They did!

Thank you, ladies for a great start of my afternoon!

location.address: 9 Rue de l'Abbé de l'Épée, 75005 Paris, France
location.name: L'Orée du Parc

properties.review_text_published:
I came out on a Wednesday night looking for a snack. I went to two places nearby. Unfortunately, things didn't work out at those and I left. I found this place around the corner. Little neighborhood joint, no frills.

What really stood out to me is the fact that tables outside are not sardines together like at a lot of bistros in Paris: you actually get space! 🚀🙏🏿😉

As you can tell, I had the beef tartare. They offer four different versions (including Thai and Italian). I had the traditional one. Solid 💪🏿🪨. Especially with a bit of Dijon mustard 😋😉. Watch out though, because theirs is 🔥: it's not for the weak.

My waiter, middle aged French guy with glasses 🕶️🤓, God bless his heart 😂, was classic Parisian: a bit standoffish, a bit sweet and caring. More so on the sweet side.

The table in front of me was playing chess. Another was having a political convo. Overall, a place with a neighborhood feel.

Keeping it hooooodd. Keeping gangsta.

Nice, no frills joint in the 5th for a night 🌃 cap.

Large, overwhelming cocktail selection. No bullsh$t bar tenders. Eclectic 🔥. Viby music. Viby atmosphere.

As the African saying goes, beware of the calm water 💦🌊, that's just where the crocodile 🐊 might hide 🙏🏿.

I came looking for a spring wide brim hat! I came with a very specific idea of what I wanted. The young lady I interacted with, Rachelle, was super attentive, thoughtful and, mostly, she listened. She was very patient and helped me to narrow down great options. Admittedly, I have a big head 🙄😂, which complicated things.

I ended up leaving with this cute, two colored panama hat 😍.

Overall, an amazing, kind and thoughtful shopping experience, in a peaceful and thoughtful setting. Everything from the lighting to the music made me feel welcome.

An inviting French Bistro with a warm, rustic, burgundy red and brown interior. We're in the 5th arrondissement, the intellectual district as they call it. The establishment has a French belle époque feel to it, with a small bar and the radio in the background playing old belle époque classics and new ones.

I was greeted and seated by a warm and smiling young lady, Gladys, who was immensely helpful and patient, both to me and people around.

I always judge a bistrot by their Steak frites, especially the flank steak, or bavette as they call it. It came with caramelized onions on top. I had it medium rare. It was cooked to perfection: the first bite, with the caramelized onions...amazing 😍🤩. Perfectly caramelized onions, not too sweet or acidic, with a hint of Italian parsley. The dressing on the salad is amazing, with tiny onions. Mayo on the side of course, and Dijon mustard.

For dessert, I had the rice pudding 🤤: caramel sauce and caramelized hazelnuts....mama mia! To my surprise, it wasn't heavy at all.

Overall, a good experience for a meal on my first evening back in Paris.

Seemed thoughtful and let a young family sit right at closing time.

2nd time: Shout out Jérémie!

I had the Perraudin combination: starter and main

Starter: vegan ravioli. Creamy but light (I found out the cream is garlic based and the filling Comté cheese 🧀; it's a dish from Grenoble). Tiny. Light olive oil 🫒 and slightly, very slightly garlicky.

Main: kidneys in mustard sauce 🫙, with steamed potatoes. This grandma's cooking right thurrr for offal lovers: this place was voted one of the best traditional spots in Paris, why order the regular dishes 🙄 when you can try something different...The mustard sauce was creamy and not too strong, strong enough for those afraid of offal taste. It had tiny little chopped onions in it that, likely because of the heat, were crunchy/Al dente 😋. I would describe the dish as having layered textures: sauce, onions, kidneys, potatoes.

location.address: 7 Rue des Quatre Vents, 75006 Paris, France
location.name: Les Artisans Lunetiers

properties.review_text_published:
Stepping into this establishment feels like stepping into an artisan's space: the setting feels rustic, yet modern, reflecting the vision of the owner Christian, who values tradition and authenticity: modernity meets classic. The lighting is warm, the soft music in the background soothing.

This is one of the most intimate shopping 🛒 experiences I've had in Paris. Christian is a true storyteller. Part history lesson, part lesson in the art of lunettes making and how it has evolved over the past 100 or so years. All thoughtfulness. Most of the brands he carries have 100+ years of history in the craft. Some of the brands he carries are Lunor, Henri Julien and Andy Wolf, who he is collaborating with now. I gladly walked out with a pair of these.

Overall, I very much appreciated the uniqueness, craftsmanship and history/education I encountered.

P.S.: He knew more about the pair I walked in with than I did 😂. He tightened them for me.

This is a cute new spot in the 5e, tucked on corner of a street. I walked in because it felt welcoming from the outside. I walked in at 19:00 on a Friday, early for Frenchies 🥖😂. I could tell I caught the lady owner of the place off guard 🤤, poor lady.

Anyhow, I sat down at a corner table next to the kitchen, being serenaded by Ella Fitzgerald and Louis Armstrong.

The lady was quite candid that she bought the place a few months ago and opened about a month ago. Come support her!

Around 20:00, the Frenchies started coming, both a family and a group of young guys "du métier"...from the trade. I could overhear the conversation from the family explaining the history of the place: it has a vibe to it.

I had the Oeuf Mayo, with the eggs cooked to perfection (slightly slightly moist in the inside, with home made mayo).

I followed it up with the faux filet steak, cooked medium rare to perfection. The meat, all French 🍖, tastes really good. Coming from the states, I appreciated the style of the fries 🍟😂 (if you know, you know... Frenchies...🙄).

I appreciated the crunchy, thick carrots 🥕 in the salad, and the tangy, mustardy feeling of the dressing.

On the way to the airport, I asked the taxi driver, Magloire, to stop by a boulangerie. It was 6:30 am. He was kind enough to oblige. I couldn't have been luckier.

The locale is small, clean and welcoming. I was helped by a young man who took his time to make me a nice Americano and an espresso for the driver.

I had the Jambon (turkey 🦃) croissant with cheese and béchamel 😋🌸🌼: it was surprisingly light. The croissant was crunchy, the béchamel both sweet and savory. It all worked together.

I also bought a chocolate 🍫 croissant 🥐, which I am saving for later! 🤤

Cute little patisserie that occupies a whole corner!

The brightest storefront in the neighborhood: it really catches your eye. Great choice of brown and gold colors that gives it a chic look. The counter looks so fancy too, bougie 🙄. It matches well with the interior and the brown of the pastries.

Their flan has won "best in Paris" many times apparently. As you can see, it is very presentable. By the time I left the store, two people walked in buying the last two pieces! It tastes yummy 😋 😋 🤤! The fact that it's encased in a soft crust makes it hold well together. I tasted hints of vanilla. It wasn't too sweet at all. It worked! It's the best and most original flan I've had.

Soothing music inside to match the interior decoration.

Americano coffee was great too.

A hole in the wall with a beautiful 😍 interior joint serving Lebanese fare!

Don't let the size fool you: really welcoming, clean and cutely decorated. The exposed walls give that oriental feel. The small number of tables in the back creates an intimate atmosphere.

I was warmly greeted by the nice man and woman at the counter.

Coming from the States, I appreciated finding menu items like Lebanese sausages that I've rarely seen even in major cities like Boston.

I had the meat pie and the sausage sandwich.

The pie 🥧 tasted amazing: the dough was soft, didn't feel heavy, and the meat was well seasoned.

My sausage sandwich came with garlic 🧄 sauce (which I tasted on the first bite), lettuce, tomato 🍅, onions, parsley and turnips. The garlic was not overpowering at all. On the second bite, I tasted the amazing sausage 🤤 😍, with a hint of smoked paprika-like heat and excellent seasoning. The pita 🫓 bread was al dente, not too soft, not too hard. Everything worked well together.

I left with a tasty 😋 baklava present: not too sweet, crunchy, with orange blossom 🌼🌸 flavor.

Living like a Doctor 🙄😂.

I had a fantastic time at this establishment! ✨

What strikes you when you walk in are a few things:

- The warmth and politeness of the hostesses! 😍 I was welcomed by Marion! I came without a reservation: upstairs was packed, so naturally I didn't mind sitting downstairs. After a while Marion came back to get me: she was so thoughtful that she arranged for a table upstairs facing the piano player. King treatment! 👑
- The music too: you are serenaded by an amazing live piano player, at least on the evening I visited 🎹
- The light and how it interacts with interior: they are a combination of soft moon-like feeling lights and candles. You'd think the place would feel dark, but no, it feels very bright. The dark marble shiny tables definitely help! ✨

Overall, the place has an unassuming elegance to it, a confidence that does not scream at you. In fact most of the people dining were dressed casually.

I had the foie gras maison: so buttery, not too strong smell-wise but strong enough, with black pepper, salt and red pepper flakes: this is one of the best I've had anywhere. 🤤

I'm a sucker for cauliflower, so I followed things up with the velouté de chou-fleur, a foamy and velvety soup with a hint of bechamel (truffle or nutmeg?)... croutons, cheese and chives. 🍲

For the coup de grâce, I had the aile de raie (Skate wing) with capers, croutons and parsley. Wow! The fish is sprinkled with rock salt and tastes so buttery... 🐟

Dig this. It's not called Bistro des Poèmes for no reason. At the end, they asked me if I wanted to write a poem that could be picked for display. I obliged. 📝

The service here is on another scale: in addition to how I was welcomed, Julia who took my order was so patient as I spent (my apologies Julia) ages making up my mind 🙄, which is so unlike me (tells you how great the menu looks). Also, I overheard Marion addressing the table next to me, one of the guests of which did not really enjoy their plate: both were so thoughtful and she addressed it by letting the chef know (she came back to tell them) and offered redress.

Overall, this place is run by very thoughtful young ladies—shout out to Marion, Maylisse, Bambou and Julia—who are griinnddiingg, grinding: come support them! 💪💖

This cute establishment looks like it is not for the weak! 😅 Come, it's super cute and friendly. If you're not from town, do something a little off the beaten path and come to this inner city part of town, Agio Ioannis specifically: it's all locals.

The establishment has an amazing terrace, which you wouldn't guess from first walking in. The whole place has the vibe of a Manu Chao song or video. It feels like a blend of Euro and Caribbean vibes in Athens. It's funny, because as I was writing this review, a Manu Chao song came on! 😯😜

The music is a nice vibe of Greek reggae, funk and other tunes: the place just feels alive, not just lively, alive, people, two amazing towering trees…🎄, the sky. Life.

I came during Greek lent and sat at the bar. The warm barman/waiter Pericles welcomed me and let me know they have a special menu, along with their usual. I ordered off the former, the baby calamari skewers 😍🔥...come on now, with picked carrots on the side, all flavor, all flavor, light on any condiments, panagia mou...I also had the marinated anchovies on a bed of fresh and luscious tomatoes 🍅.

Just do it 🙏🏿☝🏿✊🏿.

Cheap, cute and lively Vietnamese counter serving home made dishes! I came by around 13:00 and the place was packed...end of the line being outside. As you can see from the photos, they organize the food very neatly!

For lunch, they have different options involving combinations of 2, 3 dishes, all for less than 12€! Don't let the price fool you, the food and establishment feel lovely! 😍 💐

For those coming from the states, outside Vietnam, France is probably one of the best places to taste Vietnamese food (know/look up your history of France in that region 🙄🙏🏿)

I had the pork/chicken nems as an appetizer: crunchy, warm rice roll exterior! With the fish sauce, oh boy. Nems are Vietnamese style rolls made of a filling wrapped with rice flour and fried! Oh crunchiness.

As a main, I had the Cantonese rice with crispy chicken 😍. If you know your rice, you will really appreciate how the Vietnamese cook rice: I can bet this was Jasmine rice. It was just the right amount of sticky and tasted very good even without seasoning!

I closed things out with a banana beignet that tasted really nice, and a nice espresso!

Shout out to Lucie the bubbly lady behind the counter with a big big smile, who welcomed me to sit and pay later: you could tell by the interactions that many were regulars. All throughout I was made to feel super comfortable and welcome.

Overall, I had a fantastic, inexpensive meal, served with a smile 😄😁 and lots of great energy! 🙏🏿😍💙🔥

I walked by this establishment twice before finally making a reservation. What strikes you first is the placard of a cute lamb. Every time I passed, I felt an aura of intrigue, like a hidden portal lay behind the door. Oh boy, I was not disappointed!

This establishment specializes in cuisine from Crete. When you walk in, you are taken by warm rustic walls and an interior with earthy 🌍 green 💚 and brown 🤎 colors—the space has an old-world elegance to it. From the inside looking out, you notice climbing plants cascading down the many glass panels: to my taste, the combination gives a perfect balance between a dark and lit feeling.

To top it off, despite a busy afternoon and our tendency to joke around, our attendant Georgios, a high school teacher by trade, was amazingly warm, accommodating and helpful with recommendations.

We ordered several dishes:

Dolmadakias: rice wrapped in grape leaves. Here, unlike others I've had, they are bite/pinky finger sized, and I really liked that.

Roast lamb: the best I've had in Athens. The best. Look, if you prowl through my reviews, lamb is all over, especially grilled. This was roasted lamb and oh boy! 🤤

Tarama salata: creamy as hell and tasty

Santorini tomato fritters

Green salad with feta, figs, pecans, grenadine, dried raisins—one of the best salads I've had. What hit me the most were the smells when they laid most of the dishes on the table, simply amazing!

We also had cheese-filled dough rolls! 😍

I would highly recommend trying their organic wine from Crete, especially the white one. The red paired really well with the lamb.

Last but not least, the traditional Greek music just transports you to another place!

Overall, this is an elegant establishment suitable for many occasions, including dates, outings with close friends, family gatherings, and more.

Luxury, it's a particular feel, a disposition and attunement of the senses to outside stimulations. It cannot be put into words. This hotel/nansion has it.

Visual: 24/7 view of the Acropolis, stone interior, earthy colors
Acoustics: despite being by the side of the road, the rooms are very well isolated acoustically.
Tactile: lotion, warmed floors, jacuzzi, towels
Smell: lotion, bath/Jacuzzi 🤩

They use luxurious beauty products from Aqua di Parma. I consider myself a fashionista. I did not know lotion existed that could make ones skin feel a certain way.

George, one of the managers, who is constantly mentioned in the reviews, is the man! Let's face it: if you have a taste for this kind of luxury, you most likely hold yourself to a higher standard than most in other aspects of your life. You're like used to making decisions, telling people what to do .... Rarely are you able to mentally delegate. George has a knack for letting you delegate mentally to him. Case in point: it was a Saturday morning and I decide to extend my stay, contingent on being able to change my flight reservation. I was nervous the hotel would fill up. I expressed my plans to George who told me to relax, enjoy my day, leave the flight change to the next day, and that he'd make sure I have the room I want.

Knowing Athens quite well, I fulfilled my dietary needs in nearby Pagrati and other neighborhoods. Check out my other Athens reviews if you want fantastic dining experiences! 🙏🏿🌹😍

I am excited about this place because of the warmth and attention you get, without being overbearing and the luxurious feel. The shared parking space is also a great bonus.

This unassuming joint punches way above its weight class 😍🥊. I am a sucker for puns, and so was drawn to it by the play on words: meat roses.

It's on a quiet street/square in Pagrati. While unassuming, the interior is well kept, with a mix of earthy black and blue and, of course, the subtle smell of sizzling meat and roasting pork gyro.

I was greeted and assisted by a laid back gentleman, Aggelos (Angel). I ordered the pork gyro and suckling beef skewer. They ran out of the latter and substituted it for the more expensive black Angus skewer 🌹🔥. When I say 🔥, I mean it: tender, just enough fat, juicy, topped with rock salt.

I appreciated three things about their gyro. It fit in my average sized hands. The pork was 😋. The pork to fries/filling ratio was high. It was easy to eat, both from the logistical 🤣 and taste standpoints. We've all had that messy sandwich you need to finish with a knife and fork 🤣🙄.

How do I know it was good? It had that Greek gyro after taste, dig? 🤣. If you know you know. Not the overbearing, street kind. The bougie 🕯️ kind. So, it's perfect if you're into gyro gyro, your partner(s) is/are not, and y'all wanna meet half way.

They have both in and outside seating, which is convenient.

As a sign of their attention, I was busy on my phone and was advised by Angel that a beef is to be eaten warm 😋. I was also gifted a mastiha shot. Nice touch for a gyro joint, which goes back to my point of bougie but not really: it's a bougyro 🙏🏿🙄😂.
