Trust.

As you may have read in my profile description, I unapologetically choose to feature, and share with enthusiasm, about professionals that griinndddd.

Νικόλαος (Nikolas), the owner of Deli Mets is the quintessential professional, quintessential. Mets is the little hidden gem sister of the Pagrati neighborhood. Here, you get a small town vibe in the middle of Athens ☺️, and Νικόλαος' establishment is the perfect illustration of this. Don't tell anyone, especially not the hipsters. On a whim, I decided to walk around the neighborhood and take back roads to see what I might be missing. Towards the end of my stroll, I remembered that I was looking for a certain type of grain. Lucky me I happened on Deli Mets. As soon as I walked in, I knew I would have a great experience. Νικόλαος greeted me and, as a true professional, took charge and told me I could try anything I wanted. Confession: I'm a sucker for Deli stores, so his way of welcoming me made me smile inside. The first thing I tried was the truffle graviera (Greek gruyère) 😍😋🤯. I took some home. Then, we moved to cold cuts (Buffalo from the north of Greece, beef pastrami, and a chorizo like one). I took some of the last two home. Finally, he has amazing olives from Kalamata 🫒.

On top of his service, Νικόλαος seems like a super friendly and laid back person, whose passion and knowledge about his products is obvious. Delis can be overwhelming. Nicolas got you. There is a reason he is 5 star rated. Originally from Crete, you can feel his love for the island. To top off the whole experience, live traditional Greek music 🎶 playing outside serenades you. On my way out, he offered me a shot of Raki (Crete's grappa/Tsipouro). It's from his uncle 🤣😀.

Νικόλαος also tells me that he makes and sells focaccia cold cut sandwiches out of anything he sells. He also prepares boards you can either have there or to go.

All in all, I walked out €10 down with enough amazing products to share with 3 people 🤯.

Dig this. I tipped for his service. He one-upped me with a small gift. True professional.

Greeks do not get their flowers when it comes to wine. French, fine. Italian, Spanish...at the risk of offending, as a wine bar owner put it, find me any expensive such wine, I can find a much cheaper Greek wine that will go toe to toe with it. It might take beating. But it will last in the ring.

With that out of the way. I had a fantastic experience at this cave that was recommended to me by the manager of my hotel George. Eleni, a young lady with a warm and calming presence assisted. What struck me most was her patience and her ability to listen to my preferences and desire to explore Greek wine, as well as make suggestions without the least hint of being pushy.

The place itself has a well lit, expansive layout that invites you in and, to me, makes the shopping experience very friendly and undaunting, especially if you are nervous about shopping for "fancy" wine. Thoughtful and elegant describe the atmosphere well. The music is also low in volume and soothing.

As a self aware bubbles snob 😅🙄, I must admit I came in for champagne champagne, I left with a Xinomavro blanc de noirs, in the spirit of supporting Greek 🙏🏿😏. The clear bottle is from the rare Limnio grape 🍇 that folklore says Homer consumed in the form of red wine. The dark bottle is a Cretan Liatiko rosé.

I'd go with Homer any day over Molière, Cervantes, or Dante 🙄🤯.
Just saying.

A local, Greek friend had been arm twisting for weeks me to take this easy trip from Athens down the southern coast/Riviera. As they put it "you are hard to get consent from!". Well, I'm glad I finally did!

We drove there on a Saturday afternoon in late February. Once you get to Glyphada, it's all smooth, picturesque riding down the beautiful coast, with the Mediterranean in the horizon, ships and a few islands.

Arriving there, we found parking easily. During the summer, I can only imagine the challenge 🤯😅! We paid $5 a pop for the tickets, which I thought fair.

It's hard to describe the feel of this place. It's not just the monument itself, it's the earthy clay, the panoramic view of the sea and surrounding rollings hills and mountains. Picture all of this, with an ever present sky and changing light conditions! 🤯
No movie/painting can replicate this.

I would make a day trip out of it, not simply rush to get there and back. We stopped for a late lunch on the way back, a 5 minute drive from the monument, at a lovely Greek fish taverna with some pasta options.

As we drove back around dusk, you could see the imposing monument lit up in the horizon 😍. Acropolis, I don't know 😅🤣.
Trust.

I had a cheap, fantastic meal here 😋: beef in wine 🍷 sauce, with rice and roasted potatoes.

This is a no-frill neighborhood taverna, taverna. It is located in a part of town called Mets, I believe, near Pagrati, both about a 20-30 minute walk from the center. You can tell locals, mostly, frequent it: it's not for the weak 😂.

Come with a little bit of patience 🙄!

Μαύρος Γάτος (Black Cat) came highly recommended by two professionals I trust: Sakis, the Chef at Zohos, and Vagelis of Lost Roots wine bar. When these highly-rated creators point you somewhere, you go! Dig?

The moment you walk in, the space hits different: a gleaming vitrine of fine meats, walls adorned with traditional murals and artisan crafts. Old-world elegance meets timeless sophistication.

Their veal liver is a masterpiece of simplicity: wrapped in silky lamb suet, kissed with garlic olive oil and rock salt. Cut into it and the perfectly pink center releases juices that mingle with the golden oil - creating pure magic on your plate.

The busy atmosphere only adds to the experience. The staff nail that perfect Greek hospitality - attentive but natural, making sure you're having a great time in a way that feels completely unscripted.

Amaaazziing place. But it's not for the weak 🙄

P.S.: Make that reservation, especially for weekends. Trust.

Lovely coffee, sweets and nuts shops run by even lovelier people, women mostly it seems when I visited.
They were very welcoming, as were the warm earthy colors used to decorate the place.

I had an Americano and a complementary taste of their halva, which tasted buttery, smooth and out of this world, without being too sweet.

On the way out a young lady explained to me that they carry different flavors, made from tahini, honey or hazelnut 😍! They also have travel friendly already packaged products!

This place lives up to Its name! Everything from the menu to the decoration screams "Alchemist" 🔥.

Second time here, and both were a hit.

This time around, I had the canard (duck) 🦆 confit and the pommes de terre dauphinoises (soft, round sliced potatoes, baked in cream, topped with browned cheese)…all at way below Paris price points and great taste! 🤯

Bring your girl/man here, homegirl/homie 😂.

P.S.: last time, I mainly did appetizers...the Brie with truffles was 🔥

No frill fast food joint frequented by many locals.
Friendly service, huge portions: tasty pork gyros and tasty kebab.

I first ate here a year and a half ago. I came back from their coal grilled butterflied chicken thigh 🔥.

I also had the tabouleh salad 🥗, which was nice and, interestingly, was topped with feta.

This is a no frill spot, cheaper than spots around Mounastiraki and Syntagma. They seem like honest people: the fish soup I ordered tasted great but came out lukewarm. I couldn't finish it. When I asked them to take it back, they were thoughtful enough to check in and ask for honest feedback, which I gave them. We handled it like adults. I thought nothing of it. In the end, without my asking them, they took it off the bill without my asking.

I write this review of Electra Palace with great pleasure and enthusiasm!

My first impression when I walked was "I love the old world feel of this hotel, it oozes a certain level of stateliness". Little did I know that I would be in four days of being attended to and pampered like a prince! 🤴🏿. You are at the entrance of the historic Plaka neighborhood, right at the foothills of the Acropolis, a few minutes walk to Syntagma and Monasteraki squares. In addition to the hotel, you are surrounded by a number of cool and funky restaurants, wine bars, tavernas and tapas places. I really enjoyed the fact that I was not in the middle of the "touristy" part of Plaka, though it is not far from it.

I love seeing professionalism on display: I am fortunate to say that in the many years coming to Greece, I have rarely seen this level of professionalism on display, from the valet, the reception desk (shout to Mr. Stefanos), to the room service staff.

I stayed in one of the Acropolis facing suites, which I found very clean and cozy. I particularly enjoyed the luxurious feel of the marbled bathroom. The temperature control took a little bit of figuring out as I came at one of the coldest times of year. After that, it was smooth sailing. There is both a morning and an afternoon room service 🌞.

Breakfast was amazing, amazing! Shout out to Evi and the rest of the staff for their professionalism.

I would highly recommend you check out the first floor restaurant Zohos, a separate review of which I have written: attention, attentiveness and creativity on display there too!

Shout out, too, to Aleccia and Dionysus from the first floor bar.

P.S.: To management, I would suggest equipping the suites with dedicated irons and ironing boards, for those of us that are of the old school type. The rooms do come with a steamer, and every time I called for an iron and a board, they were sent over pronto.

Listen, for all of y'all that want authentic authentic authentic, come to this place in Kato Petralona.

On my fourth or fifth visit before writing this review, the seafood 🦞🍢 is of amazing quality, the portions immense.

Warning: it's not for the weak, including me 😂😊.
Greek, Greek, Greek. Αυτό είναι, ξέρεις;

First visit: Cute Greek food truly meets French Bistro! ☺️😍🔥.

Look at the presentation on this dish! 🔥

Lovely vibe, and lovely woman and young man running the place it seems 🙏🏿. On a rainy day in Athens, you brightened my day!

Third visit: Shout out to Effi and Stratos who run this lovely place! By far on my top 3 Athens favorite spots. They exude warmth, the Greek food + bistro ambiance they created just works! They are knowledgeable about wine! Pay them a visit.

This time around, I had the ribeye (entrecôte) steak 🥩, cooked, as you can see, a perfect medium rare! 😍.
First time I had a steak in Greece and they killed it! 🔥

Great steak, period. And the fries...😋🙄🙏🏿.

Shout out to George, the two ladies that attended to me, and to the rest of the team!

First of all, any restaurant with such attention to detail deserves a shout: I dropped my serviette inadvertently, which they noticed and very quickly brought me a replacement without my asking for it !

The attention to detail carries over to the elegantly decorated interior, setting and table! I love the rustic and elegant ambiance of this place!

I started with the home made bread and butter: they were both awesome. The bread reminded me of a high end version of rustic bread you might have in a village.

Then, I treated myself to two exquisite plates, namely the cod, with a tomato relish 😋 and the delightful onion tart : I would describe both as ammaazziinngly creative and unique, as I would the rest of the menu based on my reading of it. I tend to enjoy strong flavours and they, I must say, seem to have a great job at taming the flavours for those less inclined to them than I am. Shout out to the Chef for keeping the food homie and elegant!

Finally, I ended things with a scoop of fennel ice cream on top of olive crumble: it works really well!

Last but not least, I want to thank them for their attentiveness and warmth.

P.S.: The wine selection looks amazing and extensive, though I did not get a chance to try it.

location.name: I Stoa tou Psiri Η Στοά του Ψυρρή

properties.review_text_published:
Listen. This is a Gem in  Ψσυρρή (Psyrri). Not just a gem. A Gem Gem. Tucked in a block away from other establishments that might haggle you to come in. This one doesn't. The place is super clean, decorated with earthy green colors and marble: simply welcoming.

As a Chef, I agree with people raving about the chickpeas 😍. I had the smoked mackerel too, a highly underated fish in general: out of this wooorrlld, with some sliced red onions, parsley, paprika and lime: φίλλλλεεεε. The meat balls were to die for too.

❤️🫶🏿🔥

Here to show this establishment some love!

Best lamb chops I've had in Athens, and in a while anywhere, period. The fries that accompanied them were top notch too, as was the greens salad. The Ntakos salad was super fresh and tasted amazing as well, particularly the tomatoes.

This was also the best house wine I've had!

Last but not least, a very attentive yet not overbearing staff and a cute decor ❤️.

After a long afternoon of walking, a great surprising find on the way up to Plaka! This is a cute wine bar with a great selection of sparkling wines.

I was greeted by a lovely young man who recommended to me a great Greek white sparkling wine, the first I've ever had 🔥🍸.

Will return for more, mos def.

First night back in the town, Athens. Get off the plane. Shower. Smell good. Go find lamb chops. I haadd to go for the lamb chops. I haadd to 😅.

This joint is in the old part of town, Plaka. I was greeted and seated courteously on a winter evening. The chops were juicy with some bones, very good: like a bougie taverna feel. The tzatziki tasted good too, as did the fries 🔥.

location.address: 8 Holyoke St, Cambridge, MA 02138, United States
location.name: The Smoke Shop BBQ - Harvard Square

properties.review_text_published:
My favorite place in Harvard square to have a quiet meal, in a pleasant and thoughtful atmosphere: shout out to the manager for having such a polite staff.

Pictured, the brisket sandwich with extras (vegetables) and a lone bone.

location.address: 440 Somerville Ave, Somerville, MA 02143, United States
location.name: Union Square Physical Therapy

properties.review_text_published:
I had a great first session with Meagan! She seems very thoughtful and attentive. She took the time to listen to me. Most of all, she seems to really know her profession and the body. I appreciated her explaining here and there how different exercises relate to body mechanics.

Demba

location.address: 1493 Cambridge St, Cambridge, MA 02139, United States
location.name: Laura Anderson, MD

properties.review_text_published:
I had a great exchange with Dr. Anderson. She seems very thoughtful and willing to listen.

Special compliments to one of her front desk assistants, a young lady who was very professional checking me in and out, and walking me to the location of my X ray exam!

Demba.

Lovely and welcoming book store!

Shout out to Hector and the lady at the counter who advised me on cook books and wrapped them up for me!

Shout out to Ama, who comes across as an amazingly professional, creative and passionate young woman! Shout out to her for doing her thang! She seems to really have built a corner of Croissant Heaven 😍.

They specialize in pâte feuilletée. I was greeted warmly when I walked in, offered the option between two coffees described in such poetic terms ! She is a true story teller, and I loved hearing about her story and plans.

Come support her! 🙏🏿🔥

I walked into this store looking for change! To my surprise, the young lady at the counter was happy to acquiesce. She proceeded to kindly ask if I wanted a coffee, to which I agreed. I had an Americano and it was great!

I was told by a regular customer, a driver/chauffeur, that this is one of the best coffee places in town. Chauffeurs, well, go around a lot. I never understood why Greeks made such a big deal of Freddo Espresso until I had it here on my second visit!

As for the locale itself, it is beautiful: a white background with beautiful tiles, warm earthy blue touches. It is also very clean.

Shout out to Cupaki 🔥🙏🏿.

Serendipitous find while walking!

Professionally dressed, professionally run Tequila/Mezcal bar! I love to see young folks grinding 😉🔥, and these folks are doing it: one of the very few times I got served a cocktail with a napkin underneath it, and an embroidered napkin at that (pictured), while the place was packed! That's gotta count for something...!

An amazing Tequila/Mezcal list to go with it, pictured as well.

The menu feels super creative too, while sticking to the basics as well. To test whether they really on it, I had the Mezcal Negroni 🔥. Then I moved to the Lokalism, one of their cocktails where they fuse in local influence like olive oil.

The atmosphere on a Friday evening was lively and lovely 🌹.

Bravo.

I wanted to come in and show these cats a little bit of love! 💞. I enjoy this place because, to me, it feels like a French bistrot/brasserie meets Greek cuisine/flavors: they have one of the most *creative* menus I've seen in Athens.

I've eaten here on three separate occasions. The first time, I had the braised beef shoulder: some may call the portion small. It was 'the right amount' for me. On the second visit, I had a 🔥pasta with oxtails. On the day of this review, I had the braised suckling pork sandwich. On all three occasions, I had glasses of the Thymiopoulos red wine: a must try (very hard to find by the glass around). He is the guy who tamed Xinomavro, a Greek grape variety Nebiolo lovers will enjoy.

As for the service, I don't like being bothered when I eat and I had that. The servers are young, so yes, they will make young people mistakes 🤣 or faux pas: give them a little bit of a break. I've come at times when the place was not busy. I can see how, at busy times, their mistakes could be amplified.

In closing, I find Gaston better food wise than a lot of the higher rated places around 🙏🏿.

This is by far the best shave I've had in my life!I

I love seeing young people hustle: you can really tell that these young people do. From the moment you walk in, you are greeted with smiles. Their attention to detail is obvious from how meticulously the local is decorated and kept clean!

This brings us to the shave: super mad XL shout out to Thanasis. I got the beard trim/hot towel shave. I like my beard kept a certain way, and he was very attentive to my preferences. This was quite a sensory experience. It started with a head massage with some nice aromas and scents, followed by a hot towel, the blade shave, a colder towel, a final massage and finishing touches.

The music was amazingly on point too! 🔥🙏🏿❤️‍🔥

Shout out to Kostas and Dimitra! Very lovely young people running this new shop in Petralona!

Great customer service, salesmanship and very polite!

Shout to David (pictured) and Irene for being such lovely, thoughtful, smiling 😊 and warm humans 💞😍.

Shout out to the sommelière Laura for being so knowledgeable and for understanding my palette from the cryptic descriptions I gave.

By far the best wine bar I've been to: the warm ambiance, the earthy colors, the broad selection.

On one of the nights I came, David serranated the whole place with his piano 🎹 playing, and followed it up with a fire 🔥 DJ house/lounge DJ set.

location.address: Lersundi K., 3, Abando, 48009 Bilbao, Bizkaia, Spain
location.name: Restaurante Kantal Lersundi

properties.review_text_published:
Great find it Bilbao! Before coming. I called to make a reservation and got a call back to confirm. A welcoming and personable atmosphere. I started with the local mushrooms and egg yolk. The local mushrooms melt in your mouth. I followed it up with the tuna tartare and fried egg. The tuna was fresh, amazing, and the egg crruunchhhyyy 🍲☺️. I then had the dry rice with oxtails and green pepper. I've never had oxtails this soft and this smoky! It was all super tasty! Ended things with the grilled brioche desert.

Yes, I baalleed outta control! 😂

For wine, I'm a big fan of Verdejo (a local white wine)!

I had breakfast at this amazing neighborhood joint! It's run by a lovely couple. I had the "French toast" and a spicy tortilla...maaannn. The husband made me two great coffees! He also gave me an itinerary for a day trip, called to make a reservation at the joint I went to next. Mil gracias, mil gracias por la hospitalidad y el cariño. Y pues, lo del chocolate 🫕🍫...😂🙏🏿🔥

location.address: Enekuri Artxanda Errepidea, 61, 48150 Bilbao, Bizkaia, Spain
location.name: Txakoli Ballano

properties.review_text_published:
Shout out to this joint, tucked in the mountains! ❤️‍🔥

I had the best anchovies, baby shrimp and steak I've had in a while. I had the sirloin, cooked rare: the knife cut into it like it would through soft butter. The service was immensely thoughtful and warm. The day was sunny, with a light breeze. It was a good day, here, in the outskirts of Bilbao.

location.address: Mazarredo Zumarkalea, 8, Abando, 48001 Bilbao, Bizkaia, Spain
location.name: Jardines Berria

properties.review_text_published: Come with an open mind. Enjoy the warmth of the place. The less than glowing reviews may have valid points: I had a great time! This place, inside, a Mt the bar, has a super Zen, peaceful vibe ☺️🔥🌹❤️‍🔥

location.address: under railway bridge, to, 19 Vine st next, 2 America Square, London EC3N 2PX, United Kingdom
location.name: De Vine

properties.review_text_published:
Lovely, no fuss, clean, little breakfast joint!

I had the De Vine breakfast, with warm, fresh from the griddle hash browns, tasty black pudding.

Shout out to them for punching above their weight class: the breakfast was super tasty, the atmosphere chill, the service polite and courteous 🙏🏿🔥

A very homey and welcoming family-own restaurant that left me with a warm feeling inside, both literally and figuratively.

The owners felt very thoughtful. I ate here three times over the course of three days! On the first day, I had the veal special, which came in a cast iron skillet. On Friday, they have an amazing couscous special. On Monday, I had the dorade special!

🔥😍❤️‍🔥

The fondant au chocolat pictured was amazing.

The establishment seems very unassuming from the outside. The attention to detail when it comes to the food and service were amazing!

Demba.

I write this review with immense pleasure and enthusiasm! Shout out to Agata!!! :) : there's no feeling like getting off red light flight, jet lagged out of your mine, spending an hour and a half in a cab, and getting a warm welcome from a very thoughtful and kind individual, who communicates well, pays lots of attention to details and makes you feel welcome and that warm feeling inside.

I stayed in one of the suites with a separate bedroom and living room: simply gorgeous 🥰 (again, attention to detail), with thoughtful decoration, warm, earthy colors, an amazing ambiance and a bathroom with tiles to die for.

Shout out to Lionel as well, who takes over in the evening and is so amazingly jovial! 🙏🏿

If you come around, a plug for an amazing family restaurant around the corner: L'Esprit de famille ! 🥰❤️‍🔥🙏🏿😍

Demba.

Vagelis, the owner of this establishment I think, is the man! Such a thoughtful, soft spoken person, who knows a great deal about wine, especially Greek wine. He introduced me to Xinomavro, and a few other fantastic Greek wines!

Amazingly welcoming and warm bar, run by kind people. Great Americano, great chicken sandwich and chocolate/vanilla cake. I could sit here all day and just chill.

Shout out to Giorgios and Thanasia for keeping the ice cold and the coffee warm!🙏🏿

I had a fantastic meal a this joint.

I find the negative reviews a bit harsh. I'd take into account that you are right outside a meat market.

In my opinion, Valtisa rates highly in terms of authenticity, uniqueness, off the beaten pathness, deliciousness, and artistic flair!

By the first two, I'm referring to their menu: it's Cretan based and inspired Cuisine, which I think is quite challenging to find so close to the center. By the second I mean that Valtisa is in a secluded area that you would likely not venture to if you stayed in the Center. It's in Petralona, a working class neighborhood close to the center. I had a delicious Cretan Pie with fennel and a succulent Cretan lamb sausage. I plan to come back and sample the rest of the menu. I think Valtisa means suit case in Greek because the place is decorated with many many old school suitcases 😍; quite creative.

I was welcomed very warmly and told they have a super cool open air roof top, which is totally not obvious from outside! I definitely plan to come back and sample the rest of the menu 🔥🙏🏿.

Fantastic taverna on, arguably, the cutest street in Athens. I would describe the food as very true to the taverna spirit and creative at the same time: the tomato croquettes, which I found amazing (tomato sweet and sour, buttery from the batter...🤯) are a great example of this, as is the Dakos salad. The day I came, they had a lamb special for lunch: I had to ask the chef for the recipe...Overall, I found the staff thoughtful and attentive, yet not overbearing in the least. I also thought that the decor with its choice of colours (earthy red and maroon) and the ambiance exude a lot of warmth.

Shout out to Babis and his father Christos who run the place.
My room was very clean, cleaner than in some hotels I've stayed in in Athens. The staff is very hospitable. I was welcome with a nice frappé from the coffee shop next door.

This lovely restaurant is located in a working-class neighborhood of Athens called Petralona, a true neighborhood joint. The restaurant has two parts: the veranda and the inside. The atmosphere of the veranda feels lovely, backyard like. The whole restaurant has a warmth to it accentuated by the warm, earthy olive green and yellow colors that decorate it. I had amazing food: the Dakos salad, the lamb stew, which was grandmacooking  out of this world 🔥🤯, the saganaki fried cheese, and the grilled sea bass with roasted potatoes 😋🙏🏿🇬🇷.

I had a fantastic time at this establishment! I loved the warm welcome they gave me: I did walk in with a coffee and was kindly and courteously told they'd need to take it away, which I find reasonable. I was not pestered into walking in. I found the inside beautifully decorated with warm earthy colors and very clean. I found the food creative: the chicken gyro was an open plate with caramelized tomatoes and what seemed like picked onions. The table across from me seemed like they were from Greece, which speaks to the fact that this is not a 'tourist trap' despite being in the centre.

I am absolutely thrilled to write this review for Κάβα Αμπελοσοφίες in Petralona, Athens. I had the pleasure of patronizing this establishment on not just one, but two occasions, and both experiences left me with nothing but fond memories and a strong desire to return.

First and foremost, I need to express how warmly I was welcomed by Theoni, who works at Κάβα Αμπελοσοφίες. Her enthusiasm for her work and her genuine care for her customers truly stood out from the moment I walked in. She took the time to patiently answer all of my questions and taught me so much about Greek wine. Her knowledge and passion were infectious, and I felt like I was talking to a dear friend rather than just a shop assistant. She helped me select a couple of wonderful bottles and even recommended some house tsipouro, which I thoroughly enjoyed.

On my second visit to Κάβα Αμπελοσοφίες, I had the pleasure of meeting the owner, who also treated me with great warmth and hospitality. During our conversation, he mentioned that they also own a wine bar in the area, which has been receiving rave reviews. This only added to my excitement, as it's always a good sign when an establishment has multiple successes under its belt.

All in all, my experiences at Κάβα Αμπελοσοφίες were nothing short of exceptional. The warm welcome, the knowledgeable staff, and the impressive selection of wines all contributed to making my visits truly memorable. Whether you are a wine enthusiast or simply looking for a delightful experience, I highly recommend paying a visit to Κάβα Αμπελοσοφίες. I guarantee you won't be disappointed!

I am delighted to write this review for Enterprise Rent-A-Car in Athens City Center. During the past summer, I had the opportunity to rent a car twice from this location, and I must say that my experience was excellent.

On both occasions, I was greeted with exceptional service by the entire staff. They were friendly, courteous, and displayed a high level of professionalism. I would particularly like to commend Mr. George Kourlampas for his outstanding assistance. He went above and beyond to ensure my rental experience was seamless.

One aspect that stood out to me was the genuine care they took in explaining the different forms of insurance available. Mr. Kourlampas patiently answered all my questions and took the time to ensure I understood each option. This was a refreshing change from my experiences in other countries, where rental car staff often try to upsell unnecessary extras. I never once felt that George was trying to sell me anything, and he genuinely wanted to ensure I made the right decisions for my needs.

The location of Enterprise Rent-A-Car in Athens City Center is convenient, making it an ideal choice for exploring the city and its surroundings.

Based on my positive experiences, I can confidently say that if I am in Athens again, I will unquestionably choose Enterprise Rent-A-Car. Their exceptional service, especially the impeccable assistance provided by Mr. George Kourlampas, has left a lasting impression. I highly recommend this location to anyone in need of a rental car in Athens City Center.

Demba.

I had the pleasure of eating at this homey, unpretentious, establishment, with delicious traditional dishes, and one of the warmest families I met during the few weeks I spent in Athens. To Costas, Jenny, the kids, their grandma and grandpa, I feel lucky to have met you. I hope to see you again when I come back to Athens! That argni (young lamb), oh myyy 😍😍😍.

Evharisto para poli.

Demba.

I had a fantastic time at Merceri! I found the service amazingly thoughtful, kind, and the staff very willing to help and oblige. The establishment sits on my favorite street in Athens, Iraklidon. Come by, communicate with the staff, and support two amazing *women* chefs!

Demba.

location.address: C. Císter, 11, Distrito Centro, 29015 Málaga, Spain
location.name: La Cosmo | Dani Carnero | Restaurant in Málaga

properties.review_text_published:
I write this review for La Cosmo with a smile on my face! I visited with them twice while in Malaga, at lunch! The restaurant felt very peaceful. I particularly appreciated the thoughtfulness of the people that work here, from the Chef, the sous chefs, the attendants, and the people that greet you. I would describe the food as well as thoughtfully out together. I loved that every plate I tried seemed to have a combination of different flavors with every bite, almost like a story unfolding in your mouth!

Demba.

I found this place while having a gyro nearby. Sitting there at the restaurant, I walked out, looked up and this beautiful building hit me in the face, figuratively speaking of course. I looked it up and found out it serves as a co working space, one of which I needed!

Argiro and Yusef, two of the very professional people that work at this establishment greeted me. Argiro gave me a tour on the spot. The next week I signed up, which I found a breeze. Shout out to Sofia for so kindly welcoming me on my first day.

As for the building itself, I find it impressive: marb laden in the inside, soft, earthy, warm colors, and an attention to detail I very much appreciate.

Demba.

Tasty pizza/small plates, by a thoughtful and warm team, in a welcoming and lovely environment 😍.

Don't forget to try the tiramisu!

Demba.

Sitting here at this joint I found by chance, after walking through the streets of Athens in 43 degree weather at 14 00. Beat. I walk into this place, a lovely lady makes me feel so welcome. All is good.

Demba.

While visiting Athens from the US, I had the wonderful experience of having three of my designs printed on shirts by the amazing gentleman running this establishment!

I visited an hour or so before close. The gentleman explained to me kindly that he had an order to fill and, despite that, would accommodate me if I could have some patience. After taking a look at my designs and letting me pick shirts to stamp them on, he suggested places where I could get lunch and a haircut!

I came back to three beautiful, high quality shirts!

Demba.

location.address: 13 Beale St, Quincy, MA 02170, United States
location.name: Mango Mango Dessert

properties.review_text_published:
My team and I just spent a wonderful time at this establishment. The sweets and drinks tasted amazing.

We very much enjoyed the attentiveness of the young ladies working here, their attention to detail and their cleanliness.

Demba.

location.address: 6 Columbia St, Cambridge, MA 02139, United States
location.name: Praliné French Patisserie

properties.review_text_published:
I find it difficult to put memories of smells in words. One of my most vivid adolescent memories: walking into French Patisseries and taking in all the smells. And oh boy, have I walked into my fair share.

Praliné, a lovely Patisserie in our neighborhood conjures up those memories for me.

From the pastries, the color palette with which they decorated the establishment, to their cleanliness and professionalism, I very much enjoy this place!

I encourage you to visit the Belmont mother location too, and try the mille feuilles! 😍

Demba.

Two lovely and accommodating ladies run this establishment, one of them named Maggy. I loved the smell on my clothes when I got them back. They even put me in touch with a local bee keeper!

Demba.

Once I found this bakery, I went there every day.

I loved the warmth and graciousness of the ladies that run it. I really enjoyed the coffee and pastries as well!

Demba.

I very much enjoyed spending four days at this establishment! I made a booking at the last minute and got one of their deluxe apartments with city view, which I found very spacious, well decorated with warm, earthy colors and clean.

As someone who values privacy, I appreciate the fact that, unless you ask, they will service the rooms every other day (I believe?).

Shout out to Spiros at the front desk, who I had great conversations with about boxing, football and many other things. Shout out as well to Sara!

Demba.

I learned about this establishment in Naxos. The same lovely people that run it also run Yazoo in Naxos. I would describe the cuisine as nouveau Grec: tasty Greek style cooking with a modern, fine feel. Locals patronize the establishment, which speaks to its authenticity. The level of warmth and professionalism they treated me with speaks to the high standards they hold themselves to.

Demba.

I learned about this lovely establishment while in Naxos: I asked someone wearing a beautiful necklace who told me Prigipo had a store in Athens and made it a point to visit it.

A lovely young woman, Kalomiras, started the business. I met her both times I visited and have say that her and her staff, another lovely woman and a cheerful man, delivered some of most easygoing and best shopping experiences I have had recently. I bought beautiful earrings for my niece, which the owner herself designed.

I encourage you to visit, if only to experience the warmth of the establishment and how pretty the building and I interior look.

P.S.: Prigipo means "I made you a princess"

Demba.

While walking around in Athens, the Afro Cuban music from this establishment called me!

I used this an excuse to juice my phone up, which they obliged!

Two thoughtful and warm ladies work here. Shout out to them!

Demba.

location.name: Utopia Luxury Suites - Old Town, Rhodes

properties.review_text_published:
I write this review to thank Effie, Giannis and Evita for their hospitality, kindness and availability during my stay in Rhodes.As an appreciator of aesthetics, I loved their beautifully decorated rooms. The one I stayed in had a modern Ottoman feel to it, with both bright and earthy colors.

They know the area well and made great recommendations about places to eat and drink!

Demba.

I write this review for Motonaxos with lots of pleasure. I rented a 500 cc Honda motorcycle.

I loved Mrs. Maria and Elena's thoughtfulness, thoroughness and most importantly, their patience.

I enjoyed doing business with them and will definitely come back again when on the island.

Demba.

location.address: Naxos, Ag. Prokopios 843 00 Naxos, Ag. Prokopios 843 00, Greece
location.name: Art Cafe

properties.review_text_published:
I write this review, with lots of warmth and gratitude for the foks at Art Café: Taki, Kaliope, Nikos, Yorgos, and the many other people that run it.

They have a beautiful, welcoming, and genuine place: I find it hard to describe it in words. Others have mentioned the location, sunset etc...Yes, 100%.

Come connect with the people, bond, support the Arts! 🙏🏿

Demba.

location.address: 52 Irving Pl, New York, NY 10003, United States
location.name: Casa Mono

properties.review_text_published:
We had a fantastic evening with Layla, an adorable and aspiring actress!

We walked in late towards the end of the night on a Sunday evening. At first, our hostess felt a bit cold. We took it all in with a smile, understanding they may have had a long day. Then, Layla, who attended us, made our ride as smooth as a brand new Benz on a marble road, with all her enthusiasm. It just worked.

From the bone marrow, the foie gras, the lamb belly, the sardines, to the buñuelos...aïe aïe aïe. As a super taster, I loved the intricate flavor combinations: mandarine with fish, black seed and various kinds of onions and a sweet marmalade with the foie gras, the anise with the olives. Did I mention the color combinations? One of the best multi sensory experiences I have had in a while. We had a Ribera del Duero to top it all off 😍🙏🏿.

Thank you, Layla!

location.address: 22 N Loop Rd, New York, NY 10044, United States
location.name: Panorama Room

properties.review_text_published:
I write this review with great pleasure, and lots of warmth for Taylor at Panorama Room: I came in with my brother while the place bustled with a crowd, and Taylor took care of us and made our night! 😍.

Come here and let Taylor take care of you! 🙏🏿

Demba.

location.address: 69 Webster Ave, Somerville, MA 02143, United States
location.name: Webster Auto Body

properties.review_text_published:
I write this review for Webster Auto Body with great pleasure and lots of enthusiasm.

I visited with them to repair a broken sideview mirror. On the way there, I blew a tire. As soon as I arrived, Jerry Siegel, the owner I believe, greeted me, offered to make a phone call to a tire repair shop, and to install my spare wheel.

Overall, the shop has a family feel to it. I can't remember walking into a body shop where I felt treated with such courtesy.

When it came to installation, they got the part ready in a day and a half. I walked in the next day for installation, which took half an hour.

I recommend them highly.

Demba.

properties.review_text_published:
I just returned from my first class at HB, my first ever spinning class. I found the atmosphere and the vibe of the establishment welcoming. Ivo led the class I took. Ivo helped me to saddle up at the beginning, and the couple of times my feet got off the harnesses!

I will definitely return. They told me the Back Bay location offers spinning classes that spend half of the time on saddle and the other half off, with weights.

Merci!

location.address: 67 Beach Rd, Chapmans Peak, Cape Town, 7979, South Africa
location.name: Furny's Fine Wines & Taste Room

properties.review_text_published:
I write this review for Furny's with the utmost pleasure and enthusiasm. Furny's consistently left feelings of warmth on cold afternoons and nights while on a one week stay in Noordhoek. The attention to detail, the hospitality, the cordiality....what else can you ask for? Oh right, how about amaaziingg food served with a smile, a deep knowledge of ingredients and an insane wine selection?!

I first went there for a hot chocolate, which I had with a brownie (ask them to warm it up! how about Ice cream on it? They got that too). The hot chocolate came with marshmallows and tasted great. They have the best brownie I've had in a while.

The next time I went, I shared a bottle of the Newton Johnson with a friend: maann, light, playful in your mouth after you let it open up.
It reminded me of wine I had in a friend's village in Greece.

The night before I flew out, I had the pulled lamb with couscous, my friend the aubergine tagine. Both delicious!

Merci, Furny's.

properties.review_text_published:
I write this review with great pleasure and enthusiasm! I enjoyed a veggie bowl, with hummus, falafel, lettuce, tomatoes/cucumber salad and tahini.

I very much appreciated the courtesy of those who put together my bowl.

location.name: LA ROYAL

properties.review_text_published:
We had a great evening at La Royal. Congratulations to the owners for opening this sister location to Celeste. Ivan and Theo treated us really well. The person who attended our table had a lot of enthusiasm and eagerness, which may make some feel rushed, though I did not see rushing us as their intention. Perhaps the owners can communicate this to their staff.
Try the burger! Wow.
Gracias! Les agrademos mucho!

I write this review for ARTeHOTEL with greater pleasure and enthusiasm.

Hands down, the best service I have gotten at any hotel anywhere. Words cannot explain the hospitality and warmth I felt from Laura, Osvaldo, Junior ,Orlando, and the ladies who cooked breakfast.

The owners designed each room to reflect a facet of Cuban art depicted in an awesome photography books by one of the owners. The room felt very clean and well maintained.

Support them!

Les agradesco mucho a todos y todas de ARTeHOTEL.

location.address: 202 Third St, Cambridge, MA 02141, United States
location.name: Desfina Restaurant

properties.review_text_published:
I write this review of Desfina Restaurant with great pleasure and enthusiasm.

I have patronized this establishment for the past 14+ years, have gotten to respect it, the people who run it, and their vision. I have gotten to know them personally.

Their resilience over the past couple of years has particularly impressed me. The Boston Globe wrote about them.

I have tasted almost everything on the menu. I love the seasonal fried anchovies, the roasted and baked lamb, the octopus of course, the salmon. I appreciate their recent efforts to simplify the menu, modernize the website, while staying true to the taverna feel of the establishment.

However you decide to rate the establishment, give the owners' feedback. Ask for the manager, tell them what you like. Ditto if you do not enjoy your experience. We can all improve.

If you read this and go to Desfina, give the owners feedback, tell Tommy the manager Demba sent you, and enjoy a Mythos beer *on me*.

Kali orexi.

location.address: 589 8th Ave 16th Floor, New York, NY 10018, United States
location.name: Consulate General of Senegal in New York

properties.review_text_published:
Greetings, I visited the office of the consulate in mid July 2021 to renew my passport. Mr. Dia kindly informed me of the new procedures that require a carte d'idendité. Since I did not have one, Mr. Dia helped me to apply for one on the spot.

Yes, I found the office busy. I thought Mr. Dia and the other consulate workers I interacted with handled themselves with a great deal of courtesy and professionalism.

Today, I called to inquire on the status of my application. I spoke with Mr. Barro on the phone, who informed me that I could pick up my carte at my convenience, and could renew my passport then. I appreciated Mr. Barro's courtesy on the phone.

I look forward to visiting the office again.
